args.name: compression-format
args.desc: Compression format (0 = None, 1 = BC1/DXT1, 2 = BC2/DXT3, 3 = BC3/DXT5, 4 = BC3n/DXT5nm, 5 = BC4/ATI1N, 6 = BC5/ATI2N, 7 = RXGB (DXT5), 8 = Alpha Exponent (DXT5), 9 = YCoCg (DXT5), 10 = YCoCg scaled (DXT5))

args.name: mipmaps
args.desc: How to handle mipmaps (0 = No mipmaps, 1 = Generate mipmaps, 2 = Use existing mipmaps (layers)

args.name: savetype
args.desc: How to save the image (0 = selected layer, 1 = cube map, 2 = volume map, 3 = texture array

args.name: format
args.desc: Custom pixel format (0 = default, 1 = R5G6B5, 2 = RGBA4, 3 = RGB5A1, 4 = RGB10A2)

args.name: transparent-index
args.desc: Index of transparent color or -1 to disable (for indexed images only).

args.name: mipmap-filter
args.desc: Filtering to use when generating mipmaps (0 = default, 1 = nearest, 2 = box, 3 = triangle, 4 = quadratic, 5 = bspline, 6 = mitchell, 7 = lanczos, 8 = kaiser)

args.name: mipmap-wrap
args.desc: Wrap mode to use when generating mipmaps (0 = default, 1 = mirror, 2 = repeat, 3 = clamp)

args.name: srgb
args.desc: Use sRGB colorspace for gamma correction

args.name: gamma
args.desc: Gamma value to use for gamma correction (i.e. 2.2)

args.name: perceptual-metric
args.desc: Use a perceptual error metric during compression

args.name: preserve-alpha-coverage
args.desc: Preserve alpha test converage for alpha channel maps

args.name: alpha-test-threshold
args.desc: Alpha test threshold value for which alpha test converage should be preserved

file-gif-save.blurb: exports files in Compuserve GIF file format
file-gif-save.help: Export a file in Compuserve GIF format, with possible animation, transparency, and comment.  To export an animation, operate on a multi-layer file.  The plug-in will interpret <50% alpha as transparent.  When run non-interactively, the value for the comment is taken from the 'gimp-comment' parasite.

args.desc: The name of the URI to export the image in

args.name: raw-uri
args.desc: The name of the URI to export the image in

args.name: default-delay
args.desc: (animated gif) Default delay between frames in milliseconds

file-gif-save2.blurb: exports files in Compuserve GIF file format
file-gif-save2.help: Export a file in Compuserve GIF format, with possible animation, transparency, and comment.  To export an animation, operate on a multi-layer file and give the 'as-animation' parameter as TRUE.  The plug-in will interpret <50% alpha as transparent.  When run non-interactively, the value for the comment is taken from the 'gimp-comment' parasite.

args.desc: The name of the URI to export the image in

args.name: raw-uri
args.desc: The name of the URI to export the image in

file-pdf-load.help:
Loads files in Adobe's Portable Document Format. PDF is designed to be easily processed by a variety of different platforms, and is a distant cousin of PostScript.
If the PDF document has multiple pages, only the first page will be loaded. Call file_pdf_load2() to load several pages as layers.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

file-pdf-load-thumb.help: Loads a small preview of the first page of the PDF format file. Uses the embedded thumbnail if present.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

file-pdf-load2.help:
Loads files in Adobe's Portable Document Format. PDF is designed to be easily processed by a variety of different platforms, and is a distant cousin of PostScript.
This procedure adds extra parameters to file-pdf-load to open encrypted PDF and to allow multiple page loading.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

args.name: pdf-password
args.desc: The password to decrypt the encrypted PDF file

args.name: n-pages
args.desc: Number of pages to load (0 for all)

args.name: pages
args.desc: The pages to load in the expected order

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: vectorize
args.desc: Convert bitmaps to vector graphics where possible. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: ignore-hidden
args.desc: Omit hidden layers and layers with zero opacity. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: apply-masks
args.desc: Apply layer masks before saving. TRUE or FALSE (Keeping them will not change the output)

args.desc: The amount of images entered (This will be the amount of pages). 1 <= count <= MAX_PAGE_COUNT

args.desc: Input image for each page (An image can appear more than once)

args.name: vectorize
args.desc: Convert bitmaps to vector graphics where possible. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: ignore-hidden
args.desc: Omit hidden layers and layers with zero opacity. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: apply-masks
args.desc: Apply layer masks before saving. TRUE or FALSE (Keeping them will not change the output)

args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: vectorize
args.desc: Convert bitmaps to vector graphics where possible. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: ignore-hidden
args.desc: Omit hidden layers and layers with zero opacity. TRUE or FALSE

args.name: apply-masks
args.desc: Apply layer masks before saving. TRUE or FALSE (Keeping them will not change the output)

args.name: layers-as-pages
args.desc: Layers as pages (bottom layers first). TRUE or FALSE

args.name: reverse-order
args.desc: Reverse the pages order (top layers first). TRUE or FALSE

gimp-airbrush.blurb: Paint in the current brush with varying pressure. Paint application is time-dependent.
gimp-airbrush.help: This tool simulates the use of an airbrush. Paint pressure represents the relative intensity of the paint application. High pressure results in a thicker layer of paint while low pressure results in a thinner layer.

args.name: pressure
args.desc: The pressure of the airbrush strokes (0 <= pressure <= 100)

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-airbrush-default.blurb: Paint in the current brush with varying pressure. Paint application is time-dependent.
gimp-airbrush-default.help: This tool simulates the use of an airbrush. It is similar to 'gimp-airbrush' except that the pressure is derived from the airbrush tools options box. It the option has not been set the default for the option will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-attach-parasite.help: This procedure attaches a global parasite. It has no return values.

gimp-blend.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-blend' instead.
gimp-blend.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-blend' instead.

args.name: blend-mode
args.desc: The type of blend { BLEND-FG-BG-RGB (0), BLEND-FG-BG-HSV (1), BLEND-FG-TRANSPARENT (2), BLEND-CUSTOM (3) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.name: gradient-type
args.desc: The type of gradient { GRADIENT-LINEAR (0), GRADIENT-BILINEAR (1), GRADIENT-RADIAL (2), GRADIENT-SQUARE (3), GRADIENT-CONICAL-SYMMETRIC (4), GRADIENT-CONICAL-ASYMMETRIC (5), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-ANGULAR (6), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-SPHERICAL (7), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-DIMPLED (8), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-CLOCKWISE (9), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-ANTICLOCKWISE (10) }

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final blend (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: offset
args.desc: Offset relates to the starting and ending coordinates specified for the blend. This parameter is mode dependent. (offset >= 0)

args.name: repeat
args.desc: Repeat mode { REPEAT-NONE (0), REPEAT-SAWTOOTH (1), REPEAT-TRIANGULAR (2), REPEAT-TRUNCATE (3) }

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: max-depth
args.desc: Maximum recursion levels for supersampling (1 <= max-depth <= 9)

args.name: dither
args.desc: Use dithering to reduce banding (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x coordinate of this blend's starting point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y coordinate of this blend's starting point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x coordinate of this blend's ending point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y coordinate of this blend's ending point

gimp-brush-duplicate.help: This procedure creates an identical brush by a different name.

gimp-brush-get-angle.blurb: Gets the rotation angle of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-angle.help: This procedure gets the angle of rotation for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

gimp-brush-get-aspect-ratio.blurb: Gets the aspect ratio of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-aspect-ratio.help: This procedure gets the aspect ratio of a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed. The return value is a float between 0.0 and 1000.0.

gimp-brush-get-hardness.blurb: Gets the hardness of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-hardness.help: This procedure gets the hardness of a generated brush. The hardness of a brush is the amount its intensity fades at the outside edge, as a float between 0.0 and 1.0. If called for any other type of brush, the function does not succeed.

gimp-brush-get-info.blurb: Retrieves information about the specified brush.
gimp-brush-get-info.help: This procedure retrieves information about the specified brush: brush extents (width and height), color depth and mask depth.

gimp-brush-get-pixels.blurb: Retrieves information about the specified brush.
gimp-brush-get-pixels.help: This procedure retrieves information about the specified brush. This includes the brush extents (width and height) and its pixels data.

gimp-brush-get-radius.blurb: Gets the radius of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-radius.help: This procedure gets the radius value for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

gimp-brush-get-shape.blurb: Gets the shape of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-shape.help: This procedure gets the shape value for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed. The current possibilities are Circle (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_CIRCLE), Square (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_SQUARE), and Diamond (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_DIAMOND). Other shapes are likely to be added in the future.

gimp-brush-get-spacing.help: This procedure returns the spacing setting for the specified brush. The return value is an integer between 0 and 1000 which represents percentage of the maximum of the width and height of the mask.

gimp-brush-get-spikes.blurb: Gets the number of spikes for a generated brush.
gimp-brush-get-spikes.help: This procedure gets the number of spikes for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

gimp-brush-is-editable.blurb: Tests if brush can be edited.
gimp-brush-is-editable.help: Returns TRUE if you have permission to change the brush.

gimp-brush-is-generated.help: Returns TRUE if this brush is parametric, FALSE for other types.

gimp-brush-new.help: This procedure creates a new, uninitialized brush.

args.name: name
args.desc: The requested name of the new brush

args.name: new-name
args.desc: The new name of the brush

gimp-brush-set-angle.blurb: Sets the rotation angle of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-angle.help: This procedure sets the rotation angle for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

args.name: angle-in
args.desc: The desired brush rotation angle in degree

gimp-brush-set-aspect-ratio.blurb: Sets the aspect ratio of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-aspect-ratio.help: This procedure sets the aspect ratio for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed. The value should be a float between 0.0 and 1000.0.

gimp-brush-set-hardness.blurb: Sets the hardness of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-hardness.help: This procedure sets the hardness for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed. The value should be a float between 0.0 and 1.0.

gimp-brush-set-radius.blurb: Sets the radius of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-radius.help: This procedure sets the radius for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

args.name: radius-in
args.desc: The desired brush radius in pixel

gimp-brush-set-shape.blurb: Sets the shape of a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-shape.help: This procedure sets the shape value for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed. The current possibilities are Circle (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_CIRCLE), Square (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_SQUARE), and Diamond (GIMP_BRUSH_GENERATED_DIAMOND). Other shapes are likely to be added in the future.

args.name: shape-in
args.desc: The brush shape { BRUSH-GENERATED-CIRCLE (0), BRUSH-GENERATED-SQUARE (1), BRUSH-GENERATED-DIAMOND (2) }

gimp-brush-set-spacing.help: This procedure modifies the spacing setting for the specified brush. The value should be a integer between 0 and 1000.

gimp-brush-set-spikes.blurb: Sets the number of spikes for a generated brush.
gimp-brush-set-spikes.help: This procedure sets the number of spikes for a generated brush. If called for any other type of brush, it does not succeed.

gimp-brushes-close-popup.help: This procedure closes an opened brush selection dialog.

args.name: brush-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this pop-up

args.name: name
args.desc: The brush name ("" means current active brush)

gimp-brushes-get-list.blurb: Retrieve a complete listing of the available brushes.
gimp-brushes-get-list.help: This procedure returns a complete listing of available GIMP brushes. Each name returned can be used as input to the 'gimp-context-set-brush' procedure.

args.name: filter
args.desc: An optional regular expression used to filter the list

args.desc: The opacity of the final bucket fill (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: The threshold determines how extensive the seed fill will be. It's value is specified in terms of intensity levels. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image. (0 <= threshold <= 255)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

gimp-buffer-delete.help: This procedure deletes a named buffer.

gimp-buffer-get-bytes.help: This procedure retrieves the specified named buffer's bytes.

gimp-buffer-get-height.help: This procedure retrieves the specified named buffer's height.

gimp-buffer-get-image-type.help: This procedure retrieves the specified named buffer's image type.

gimp-buffer-get-width.help: This procedure retrieves the specified named buffer's width.

gimp-buffer-rename.help: This procedure renames a named buffer.

gimp-buffers-get-list.help: This procedure returns a complete listing of available named buffers.

args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.desc: Radius for feather operation in Y direction (feather-radius-y >= 0)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.desc: Whether to consider transparent pixels for selection. If TRUE, transparency is considered as a unique selectable color. (TRUE or FALSE)

SELECT_CRITERION_COMPOSITE is the standard choice. { SELECT-CRITERION-COMPOSITE (0), SELECT-CRITERION-R (1), SELECT-CRITERION-G (2), SELECT-CRITERION-B (3), SELECT-CRITERION-H (4), SELECT-CRITERION-S (5), SELECT-CRITERION-V (6), SELECT-CRITERION-A (7), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-L (8), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-C (9), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-H (10) }

gimp-channel-combine-masks.help: This procedure combines two channel masks. The result is stored in the first channel.

args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: offx
args.desc: x offset between upper left corner of channels: (second - first)

args.name: offy
args.desc: y offset between upper left corner of channels: (second - first)

gimp-channel-copy.help:
This procedure copies the specified channel and returns the copy.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'.

gimp-channel-delete.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.
gimp-channel-delete.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.

gimp-channel-get-color.blurb: Get the compositing color of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-color.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's compositing color.

gimp-channel-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-channel-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-channel-get-opacity.blurb: Get the opacity of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-opacity.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's opacity.

gimp-channel-get-show-masked.blurb: Get the composite method of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-get-show-masked.help: This procedure returns the specified channel's composite method. If it is TRUE, then the channel is composited with the image so that masked regions are shown. Otherwise, selected regions are shown.

gimp-channel-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-channel-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-channel-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-channel-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-channel-new.help:
This procedure creates a new channel with the specified width, height, name, opacity and color.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'. Other attributes, such as channel visibility, should be set with explicit procedure calls.
The channel's contents are undefined initially.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the channel

gimp-channel-new-from-component.blurb: Create a new channel from a color component
gimp-channel-new-from-component.help:
This procedure creates a new channel from a color component.
The new channel still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new channel with 'gimp-image-insert-channel'. Other attributes, such as channel visibility, should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the channel

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }
