gimp-channel-set-opacity.blurb: Set the opacity of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-set-opacity.help: This procedure sets the specified channel's opacity.

gimp-channel-set-show-masked.blurb: Set the composite method of the specified channel.
gimp-channel-set-show-masked.help: This procedure sets the specified channel's composite method. If it is TRUE, then the channel is composited with the image so that masked regions are shown. Otherwise, selected regions are shown.

args.name: show-masked
args.desc: The new channel composite method (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-channel-set-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.
gimp-channel-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-channel-set-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.
gimp-channel-set-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-clone.blurb: Clone from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-clone.help: This tool clones (copies) from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. If the "clone_type" argument is set to PATTERN-CLONE, then the current pattern is used as the source and the "src_drawable" argument is ignored. Pattern cloning assumes a tileable pattern and mods the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets with the width and height of the pattern. For image cloning, if the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets exceeds the extents of the src drawable, then no paint is transferred. The clone tool is capable of transforming between any image types including RGB->Indexed--although converting from any type to indexed is significantly slower.

args.name: clone-type
args.desc: The type of clone { CLONE-IMAGE (0), CLONE-PATTERN (1) }

args.name: src-x
args.desc: The x coordinate in the source image

args.name: src-y
args.desc: The y coordinate in the source image

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-clone-default.blurb: Clone from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-clone-default.help: This tool clones (copies) from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-clone' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the clones option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-color-balance.blurb: Modify the color balance of the specified drawable.
gimp-color-balance.help:
Modify the color balance of the specified drawable. There are three axis which can be modified: cyan-red, magenta-green, and yellow-blue. Negative values increase the amount of the former, positive values increase the amount of the latter. Color balance can be controlled with the 'transfer_mode' setting, which allows shadows, mid-tones, and highlights in an image to be affected differently. The 'preserve-lum' parameter, if TRUE, ensures that the luminosity of each pixel remains fixed.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-color-color-balance' instead.

args.name: preserve-lum
args.desc: Preserve luminosity values at each pixel (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-color-picker.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-pick-color' instead.
gimp-color-picker.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-pick-color' instead.

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: sample-average
args.desc: Average the color of all the pixels in a specified radius (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: average-radius
args.desc: The radius of pixels to average (average-radius >= 0)

gimp-context-get-antialias.help: This procedure returns the antialias setting.

gimp-context-get-background.blurb: Get the current GIMP background color.
gimp-context-get-background.help: This procedure returns the current GIMP background color. The background color is used in a variety of tools such as blending, erasing (with non-alpha images), and image filling.

gimp-context-get-brush.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active brush. All paint operations and stroke operations use this brush to control the application of paint to the image.

gimp-context-get-brush-angle.help: Set the angle in degrees for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-brush-aspect-ratio.help: Set the aspect ratio for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-brush-force.blurb: Get brush force in paint options.
gimp-context-get-brush-force.help: Get the brush application force for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-brush-hardness.blurb: Get brush hardness in paint options.
gimp-context-get-brush-hardness.help: Get the brush hardness for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-brush-size.help: Get the brush size in pixels for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-brush-spacing.blurb: Get brush spacing as percent of size.
gimp-context-get-brush-spacing.help: Get the brush spacing as percent of size for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-get-diagonal-neighbors.help: This procedure returns the diagonal neighbors setting.

gimp-context-get-distance-metric.blurb: Get the distance metric used in some computations.
gimp-context-get-distance-metric.help: This procedure returns the distance metric in the current context. See 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric' to know more about its usage.

gimp-context-get-dynamics.blurb: Retrieve the currently active paint dynamics.
gimp-context-get-dynamics.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active paint dynamics. All paint operations and stroke operations use this paint dynamics to control the application of paint to the image.

gimp-context-get-feather.help: This procedure returns the feather setting.

gimp-context-get-feather-radius.help: This procedure returns the feather radius setting.

gimp-context-get-font.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active font.

gimp-context-get-foreground.blurb: Get the current GIMP foreground color.
gimp-context-get-foreground.help: This procedure returns the current GIMP foreground color. The foreground color is used in a variety of tools such as paint tools, blending, and bucket fill.

gimp-context-get-gradient.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active gradient.

gimp-context-get-gradient-blend-color-sp.blurb: Get the gradient blend color space.
gimp-context-get-gradient-blend-color-sp.help: Get the gradient blend color space for paint tools and the gradient tool.

gimp-context-get-gradient-repeat-mode.help: Get the gradient repeat mode for paint tools and the gradient tool.

gimp-context-get-gradient-reverse.help: Get the gradient reverse setting for paint tools and the gradient tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-angle.help: Get the ink angle in degrees for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-blob-angle.blurb: Get ink blob angle in degrees.
gimp-context-get-ink-blob-angle.help: Get the ink blob angle in degrees for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-blob-aspect-ratio.help: Get the ink blob aspect ratio for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-blob-type.help: Get the ink blob type for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-size.blurb: Get ink blob size in pixels.
gimp-context-get-ink-size.help: Get the ink blob size in pixels for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-size-sensitivity.help: Get the ink size sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-speed-sensitivity.help: Get the ink speed sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-ink-tilt-sensitivity.help: Get the ink tilt sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-get-interpolation.help: This procedure returns the interpolation setting. The return value is an integer which corresponds to the values listed in the argument description. If the interpolation has not been set explicitly by 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', the default interpolation set in gimprc will be used.

gimp-context-get-line-cap-style.blurb: Get the line cap style setting.
gimp-context-get-line-cap-style.help: This procedure returns the line cap style setting.

gimp-context-get-line-dash-offset.blurb: Get the line dash offset setting.
gimp-context-get-line-dash-offset.help: This procedure returns the line dash offset setting.

gimp-context-get-line-dash-pattern.blurb: Get the line dash pattern setting.
gimp-context-get-line-dash-pattern.help: This procedure returns the line dash pattern setting.

gimp-context-get-line-join-style.blurb: Get the line join style setting.
gimp-context-get-line-join-style.help: This procedure returns the line join style setting.

gimp-context-get-line-miter-limit.blurb: Get the line miter limit setting.
gimp-context-get-line-miter-limit.help: This procedure returns the line miter limit setting.

gimp-context-get-line-width.help: This procedure returns the line width setting.

gimp-context-get-line-width-unit.blurb: Get the line width unit setting.
gimp-context-get-line-width-unit.help: This procedure returns the line width unit setting.

gimp-context-get-mypaint-brush.blurb: Retrieve the currently active MyPaint brush.
gimp-context-get-mypaint-brush.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active MyPaint brush.

gimp-context-get-opacity.help: This procedure returns the opacity setting. The return value is a floating point number between 0 and 100.

gimp-context-get-paint-method.blurb: Retrieve the currently active paint method.
gimp-context-get-paint-method.help: This procedure returns the name of the currently active paint method.

gimp-context-get-paint-mode.help: This procedure returns the paint-mode setting. The return value is an integer which corresponds to the values listed in the argument description.

gimp-context-get-palette.help: This procedure returns the name of the the currently active palette.

gimp-context-get-pattern.help: This procedure returns name of the the currently active pattern. All clone and bucket-fill operations with patterns will use this pattern to control the application of paint to the image.

gimp-context-get-sample-criterion.help: This procedure returns the sample criterion setting.

gimp-context-get-sample-merged.help: This procedure returns the sample merged setting.

gimp-context-get-sample-threshold.help: This procedure returns the sample threshold setting.

gimp-context-get-sample-threshold-int.blurb: Get the sample threshold setting as an integer value.
gimp-context-get-sample-threshold-int.help: This procedure returns the sample threshold setting as an integer value. See 'gimp-context-get-sample-threshold'.

gimp-context-get-sample-transparent.help: This procedure returns the sample transparent setting.

gimp-context-get-stroke-method.blurb: Retrieve the currently active stroke method.
gimp-context-get-stroke-method.help: This procedure returns the currently active stroke method.

gimp-context-get-transform-direction.help: This procedure returns the transform direction. The return value is an integer which corresponds to the values listed in the argument description.

gimp-context-get-transform-recursion.blurb: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.
gimp-context-get-transform-recursion.help: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-context-get-transform-resize.help: This procedure returns the transform resize setting. The return value is an integer which corresponds to the values listed in the argument description.

gimp-context-list-paint-methods.help: This procedure lists the names of the available paint methods. Any of the results can be used for 'gimp-context-set-paint-method'.

gimp-context-pop.blurb: Pops the topmost context from the plug-in's context stack.
gimp-context-pop.help: This procedure removes the topmost context from the plug-in's context stack. The context that was active before the corresponding call to 'gimp-context-push' becomes the new current context of the plug-in.

gimp-context-push.blurb: Pushes a context to the top of the plug-in's context stack.
gimp-context-push.help: This procedure creates a new context by copying the current context. This copy becomes the new current context for the calling plug-in until it is popped again using 'gimp-context-pop'.

gimp-context-set-antialias.help:
This procedure modifies the antialias setting. If antialiasing is turned on, the edges of selected region will contain intermediate values which give the appearance of a sharper, less pixelized edge. This should be set as TRUE most of the time unless a binary-only selection is wanted.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-image-select-round-rectangle', 'gimp-image-select-ellipse', 'gimp-image-select-polygon', 'gimp-image-select-item', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection'.

gimp-context-set-background.blurb: Set the current GIMP background color.
gimp-context-set-background.help: This procedure sets the current GIMP background color. After this is set, operations which use background such as blending, filling images, clearing, and erasing (in non-alpha images) will use the new value.

gimp-context-set-brush.blurb: Set the specified brush as the active brush.
gimp-context-set-brush.help: This procedure allows the active brush to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed brushes. If there is no matching brush found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified brush becomes active and will be used in all subsequent paint operations.

gimp-context-set-brush-angle.help: Set the angle in degrees for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-aspect-ratio.help: Set the aspect ratio for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-default-hardness.blurb: Set brush spacing to its default.
gimp-context-set-brush-default-hardness.help: Set the brush spacing to the default for paintbrush, airbrush, or pencil tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-default-size.blurb: Set brush size to its default.
gimp-context-set-brush-default-size.help: Set the brush size to the default (max of width and height) for paintbrush, airbrush, or pencil tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-default-spacing.blurb: Set brush spacing to its default.
gimp-context-set-brush-default-spacing.help: Set the brush spacing to the default for paintbrush, airbrush, or pencil tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-force.help: Set the brush application force for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-hardness.help: Set the brush hardness for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-size.help: Set the brush size in pixels for brush based paint tools.

gimp-context-set-brush-spacing.blurb: Set brush spacing as percent of size.
gimp-context-set-brush-spacing.help: Set the brush spacing as percent of size for brush based paint tools.

args.name: spacing
args.desc: Brush spacing as fraction of size (0.01 <= spacing <= 50)

gimp-context-set-default-colors.blurb: Set the current GIMP foreground and background colors to black and white.
gimp-context-set-default-colors.help: This procedure sets the current GIMP foreground and background colors to their initial default values, black and white.

gimp-context-set-defaults.blurb: Reset context settings to their default values.
gimp-context-set-defaults.help: This procedure resets context settings used by various procedures to their default value. This procedure will usually be called after a context push so that a script which calls procedures affected by context settings will not be affected by changes in the global context.

gimp-context-set-diagonal-neighbors.help:
This procedure modifies the diagonal neighbors setting. If the affected region of an operation is based on a seed point, like when doing a seed fill, then, when this setting is TRUE, all eight neighbors of each pixel are considered when calculating the affected region; in contrast, when this setting is FALSE, only the four orthogonal neighbors of each pixel are considered.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill'.

args.name: diagonal-neighbors
args.desc: The diagonal neighbors setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-context-set-distance-metric.blurb: Set the distance metric used in some computations.
gimp-context-set-distance-metric.help:
This procedure modifies the distance metric used in some computations, such as 'gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill'. In particular, it does not change the metric used in generic distance computation on canvas, as in the Measure tool.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill'.

args.name: metric
args.desc: The distance metric { Euclidean (0), Manhattan (1), Chebyshev (2) }

gimp-context-set-dynamics.blurb: Set the specified paint dynamics as the active paint dynamics.
gimp-context-set-dynamics.help: This procedure allows the active paint dynamics to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed paint dynamics. If there is no matching paint dynamics found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified paint dynamics becomes active and will be used in all subsequent paint operations.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the paint dynamics

gimp-context-set-feather.help:
This procedure modifies the feather setting. If the feather option is enabled, selections will be blurred before combining. The blur is a gaussian blur; its radii can be controlled using 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-image-select-rectangle', 'gimp-image-select-round-rectangle', 'gimp-image-select-ellipse', 'gimp-image-select-polygon', 'gimp-image-select-item'.

gimp-context-set-feather-radius.help:
This procedure modifies the feather radius setting.

This setting affects all procedures that are affected by 'gimp-context-set-feather'.

gimp-context-set-font.blurb: Set the specified font as the active font.
gimp-context-set-font.help: This procedure allows the active font to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed fonts. If no matching font is found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified font becomes active and will be used in all subsequent font operations.

gimp-context-set-foreground.blurb: Set the current GIMP foreground color.
gimp-context-set-foreground.help: This procedure sets the current GIMP foreground color. After this is set, operations which use foreground such as paint tools, blending, and bucket fill will use the new value.

gimp-context-set-gradient.blurb: Sets the specified gradient as the active gradient.
gimp-context-set-gradient.help: This procedure lets you set the specified gradient as the active or "current" one. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the loaded gradients. If no matching gradient is found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified gradient will become active and will be used for subsequent custom gradient operations.

gimp-context-set-gradient-blend-color-sp.blurb: Set the gradient blend color space.
gimp-context-set-gradient-blend-color-sp.help: Set the gradient blend color space for paint tools and the gradient tool.

args.name: blend-color-space
args.desc: Blend color space { GRADIENT-BLEND-RGB-PERCEPTUAL (0), GRADIENT-BLEND-RGB-LINEAR (1), GRADIENT-BLEND-CIE-LAB (2) }

gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-hsv-ccw.blurb: Sets the built-in FG-BG HSV (ccw) gradient as the active gradient.
gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-hsv-ccw.help: This procedure sets the built-in FG-BG HSV (ccw) gradient as the active gradient. The gradient will be used for subsequent gradient operations.

gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-hsv-cw.blurb: Sets the built-in FG-BG HSV (cw) gradient as the active gradient.
gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-hsv-cw.help: This procedure sets the built-in FG-BG HSV (cw) gradient as the active gradient. The gradient will be used for subsequent gradient operations.

gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-rgb.blurb: Sets the built-in FG-BG RGB gradient as the active gradient.
gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-bg-rgb.help: This procedure sets the built-in FG-BG RGB gradient as the active gradient. The gradient will be used for subsequent gradient operations.

gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-transparent.blurb: Sets the built-in FG-Transparent gradient as the active gradient.
gimp-context-set-gradient-fg-transparent.help: This procedure sets the built-in FG-Transparent gradient as the active gradient. The gradient will be used for subsequent gradient operations.

gimp-context-set-gradient-repeat-mode.help: Set the gradient repeat mode for paint tools and the gradient tool.

args.name: repeat-mode
args.desc: Repeat mode { REPEAT-NONE (0), REPEAT-SAWTOOTH (1), REPEAT-TRIANGULAR (2), REPEAT-TRUNCATE (3) }

gimp-context-set-gradient-reverse.help: Set the gradient reverse setting for paint tools and the gradient tool.

gimp-context-set-ink-angle.help: Set the ink angle in degrees for ink tool.

gimp-context-set-ink-blob-angle.blurb: Set ink blob angle in degrees.
gimp-context-set-ink-blob-angle.help: Set the ink blob angle in degrees for ink tool.

args.name: angle
args.desc: ink blob angle in degrees (-180 <= angle <= 180)

gimp-context-set-ink-blob-aspect-ratio.help: Set the ink blob aspect ratio for ink tool.

gimp-context-set-ink-blob-type.help: Set the ink blob type for ink tool.

args.name: type
args.desc: Ink blob type { INK-BLOB-TYPE-CIRCLE (0), INK-BLOB-TYPE-SQUARE (1), INK-BLOB-TYPE-DIAMOND (2) }

gimp-context-set-ink-size.blurb: Set ink blob size in pixels.
gimp-context-set-ink-size.help: Set the ink blob size in pixels for ink tool.

args.name: size
args.desc: ink blob size in pixels (0 <= size <= 200)

gimp-context-set-ink-size-sensitivity.help: Set the ink size sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-set-ink-speed-sensitivity.help: Set the ink speed sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-set-ink-tilt-sensitivity.help: Set the ink tilt sensitivity for ink tool.

gimp-context-set-interpolation.help:
This procedure modifies the interpolation setting.

This setting affects affects the following procedures: 'gimp-item-transform-flip', 'gimp-item-transform-perspective', 'gimp-item-transform-rotate', 'gimp-item-transform-scale', 'gimp-item-transform-shear', 'gimp-item-transform-2d', 'gimp-item-transform-matrix', 'gimp-image-scale', 'gimp-layer-scale'.

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: The interpolation type { INTERPOLATION-NONE (0), INTERPOLATION-LINEAR (1), INTERPOLATION-CUBIC (2), INTERPOLATION-NOHALO (3), INTERPOLATION-LOHALO (4) }

gimp-context-set-line-cap-style.blurb: Set the line cap style setting.
gimp-context-set-line-cap-style.help:
This procedure modifies the line cap style setting for stroking lines.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

args.name: cap-style
args.desc: The line cap style setting { CAP-BUTT (0), CAP-ROUND (1), CAP-SQUARE (2) }

gimp-context-set-line-dash-offset.blurb: Set the line dash offset setting.
gimp-context-set-line-dash-offset.help:
This procedure modifies the line dash offset setting for stroking lines.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

args.name: dash-offset
args.desc: The line dash offset setting (0 <= dash-offset <= 100)

gimp-context-set-line-dash-pattern.blurb: Set the line dash pattern setting.
gimp-context-set-line-dash-pattern.help:
This procedure modifies the line dash pattern setting for stroking lines.

The unit of the dash pattern segments is the actual line width used for the stroke operation, in other words a segment length of 1.0 results in a square segment shape (or gap shape).

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection-', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

args.name: num-dashes
args.desc: The number of dashes in the dash_pattern array (num-dashes >= 0)

gimp-context-set-line-join-style.blurb: Set the line join style setting.
gimp-context-set-line-join-style.help:
This procedure modifies the line join style setting for stroking lines.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

args.name: join-style
args.desc: The line join style setting { JOIN-MITER (0), JOIN-ROUND (1), JOIN-BEVEL (2) }

gimp-context-set-line-miter-limit.blurb: Set the line miter limit setting.
gimp-context-set-line-miter-limit.help:
This procedure modifies the line miter limit setting for stroking lines.
A mitered join is converted to a bevelled join if the miter would extend to a distance of more than (miter-limit * line-width) from the actual join point.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

args.name: miter-limit
args.desc: The line miter limit setting (0 <= miter-limit <= 100)

gimp-context-set-line-width.help:
This procedure modifies the line width setting for stroking lines.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

gimp-context-set-line-width-unit.blurb: Set the line width unit setting.
gimp-context-set-line-width-unit.help:
This procedure modifies the line width unit setting for stroking lines.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection', 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item'.

gimp-context-set-mypaint-brush.blurb: Set the specified MyPaint brush as the active MyPaint brush.
gimp-context-set-mypaint-brush.help: This procedure allows the active MyPaint brush to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed MyPaint brushes. If there is no matching MyPaint brush found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified MyPaint brush becomes active and will be used in all subsequent MyPaint paint operations.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the MyPaint brush

gimp-context-set-opacity.help: This procedure modifies the opacity setting. The value should be a floating point number between 0 and 100.

gimp-context-set-paint-method.blurb: Set the specified paint method as the active paint method.
gimp-context-set-paint-method.help: This procedure allows the active paint method to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the available paint methods. If there is no matching method found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified method becomes active and will be used in all subsequent paint operations.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the paint method

gimp-context-set-paint-mode.help: This procedure modifies the paint_mode setting.

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

gimp-context-set-palette.blurb: Set the specified palette as the active palette.
gimp-context-set-palette.help: This procedure allows the active palette to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed palettes. If no matching palette is found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified palette becomes active and will be used in all subsequent palette operations.

gimp-context-set-pattern.blurb: Set the specified pattern as the active pattern.
gimp-context-set-pattern.help: This procedure allows the active pattern to be set by specifying its name. The name is simply a string which corresponds to one of the names of the installed patterns. If there is no matching pattern found, this procedure will return an error. Otherwise, the specified pattern becomes active and will be used in all subsequent paint operations.

gimp-context-set-sample-criterion.help:
This procedure modifies the sample criterion setting. If an operation depends on the colors of the pixels present in a drawable, like when doing a seed fill, this setting controls how color similarity is determined. SELECT_CRITERION_COMPOSITE is the default value.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill'.

args.name: sample-criterion
args.desc: The sample criterion setting { SELECT-CRITERION-COMPOSITE (0), SELECT-CRITERION-R (1), SELECT-CRITERION-G (2), SELECT-CRITERION-B (3), SELECT-CRITERION-H (4), SELECT-CRITERION-S (5), SELECT-CRITERION-V (6), SELECT-CRITERION-A (7), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-L (8), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-C (9), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-H (10) }

gimp-context-set-sample-merged.help:
This procedure modifies the sample merged setting. If an operation depends on the colors of the pixels present in a drawable, like when doing a seed fill, this setting controls whether the pixel data from the specified drawable is used ('sample-merged' is FALSE), or the pixel data from the composite image ('sample-merged' is TRUE. This is equivalent to sampling for colors after merging all visible layers).

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill'.

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: The sample merged setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-context-set-sample-threshold.help:
This procedure modifies the sample threshold setting. If an operation depends on the colors of the pixels present in a drawable, like when doing a seed fill, this setting controls what is "sufficiently close" to be considered a similar color. If the sample threshold has not been set explicitly, the default threshold set in gimprc will be used.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill'.

gimp-context-set-sample-threshold-int.blurb: Set the sample threshold setting as an integer value.
gimp-context-set-sample-threshold-int.help: This procedure modifies the sample threshold setting as an integer value. See 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold'.

gimp-context-set-sample-transparent.help:
This procedure modifies the sample transparent setting. If an operation depends on the colors of the pixels present in a drawable, like when doing a seed fill, this setting controls whether transparency is considered to be a unique selectable color. When this setting is TRUE, transparent areas can be selected or filled.

This setting affects the following procedures: 'gimp-image-select-color', 'gimp-image-select-contiguous-color', 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill'.

args.name: sample-transparent
args.desc: The sample transparent setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-context-set-stroke-method.blurb: Set the specified stroke method as the active stroke method.
gimp-context-set-stroke-method.help: This procedure set the specified stroke method as the active stroke method. The new method will be used in all subsequent stroke operations.

args.name: stroke-method
args.desc: The new stroke method { STROKE-LINE (0), STROKE-PAINT-METHOD (1) }

gimp-context-set-transform-direction.help:
This procedure modifies the transform direction setting.

This setting affects affects the following procedures: 'gimp-item-transform-flip', 'gimp-item-transform-perspective', 'gimp-item-transform-rotate', 'gimp-item-transform-scale', 'gimp-item-transform-shear', 'gimp-item-transform-2d', 'gimp-item-transform-matrix'.

gimp-context-set-transform-recursion.blurb: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.
gimp-context-set-transform-recursion.help: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-context-set-transform-resize.help:
This procedure modifies the transform resize setting. When transforming pixels, if the result of a transform operation has a different size than the original area, this setting determines how the resulting area is sized.

This setting affects affects the following procedures: 'gimp-item-transform-flip', 'gimp-item-transform-flip-simple', 'gimp-item-transform-perspective', 'gimp-item-transform-rotate', 'gimp-item-transform-rotate-simple', 'gimp-item-transform-scale', 'gimp-item-transform-shear', 'gimp-item-transform-2d', 'gimp-item-transform-matrix'.

args.name: transform-resize
args.desc: The transform resize type { TRANSFORM-RESIZE-ADJUST (0), TRANSFORM-RESIZE-CLIP (1), TRANSFORM-RESIZE-CROP (2), TRANSFORM-RESIZE-CROP-WITH-ASPECT (3) }

gimp-context-swap-colors.blurb: Swap the current GIMP foreground and background colors.
gimp-context-swap-colors.help: This procedure swaps the current GIMP foreground and background colors, so that the new foreground color becomes the old background color and vice versa.

gimp-convert-grayscale.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-grayscale' instead.
gimp-convert-grayscale.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-grayscale' instead.

gimp-convert-indexed.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-indexed' instead.
gimp-convert-indexed.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-indexed' instead.

args.name: dither-type
args.desc: The dither type to use { CONVERT-DITHER-NONE (0), CONVERT-DITHER-FS (1), CONVERT-DITHER-FS-LOWBLEED (2), CONVERT-DITHER-FIXED (3) }

args.name: palette-type
args.desc: The type of palette to use { CONVERT-PALETTE-GENERATE (0), CONVERT-PALETTE-WEB (2), CONVERT-PALETTE-MONO (3), CONVERT-PALETTE-CUSTOM (4) }

args.name: num-cols
args.desc: The number of colors to quantize to, ignored unless (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_GENERATE)

args.name: alpha-dither
args.desc: Dither transparency to fake partial opacity (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: remove-unused
args.desc: Remove unused or duplicate color entries from final palette, ignored if (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_GENERATE) (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: palette
args.desc: The name of the custom palette to use, ignored unless (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_CUSTOM)

gimp-convert-rgb.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-rgb' instead.
gimp-convert-rgb.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-convert-rgb' instead.

gimp-convolve.blurb: Convolve (Blur, Sharpen) using the current brush.
gimp-convolve.help: This tool convolves the specified drawable with either a sharpening or blurring kernel. The pressure parameter controls the magnitude of the operation. Like the paintbrush, this tool linearly interpolates between the specified stroke coordinates.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-convolve-default.blurb: Convolve (Blur, Sharpen) using the current brush.
gimp-convolve-default.help: This tool convolves the specified drawable with either a sharpening or blurring kernel. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-convolve' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the convolve option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-crop.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-crop' instead.
gimp-crop.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-crop' instead.

args.name: new-width
args.desc: New image width: (0 < new_width <= width) (1 <= new-width <= 524288)

args.name: new-height
args.desc: New image height: (0 < new_height <= height) (1 <= new-height <= 524288)

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: num-bytes
args.desc: The number of bytes in the new curve (always 256) (num-bytes >= 0)

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: num-points
args.desc: The number of values in the control point array (4 <= num-points <= 34)

args.name: control-pts
args.desc: The spline control points: { cp1.x, cp1.y, cp2.x, cp2.y, ... }

gimp-debug-timer-end.help:
This procedure stops the timer started by a previous 'gimp-debug-timer-start' call, and prints and returns the elapsed time.
If there was already an active timer at the time of corresponding call to 'gimp-debug-timer-start', a dummy value is returned.

This is a debug utility procedure. It is subject to change at any point, and should not be used in production.

gimp-debug-timer-start.help:
This procedure starts a timer, measuring the elapsed time since the call. Each call to this procedure should be matched by a call to 'gimp-debug-timer-end', which returns the elapsed time.
If there is already an active timer, it is not affected by the call, however, a matching 'gimp-debug-timer-end' call is still required.

This is a debug utility procedure. It is subject to change at any point, and should not be used in production.

args.name: desaturate-mode
args.desc: The formula to use to desaturate { DESATURATE-LIGHTNESS (0), DESATURATE-LUMA (1), DESATURATE-AVERAGE (2), DESATURATE-LUMINANCE (3), DESATURATE-VALUE (4) }

gimp-detach-parasite.help: This procedure detaches a global parasite from. It has no return values.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach.

gimp-display-delete.help: This procedure removes the specified display. If this is the last remaining display for the underlying image, then the image is deleted also. Note that the display is closed no matter if the image is dirty or not. Better save the image before calling this procedure.

gimp-display-get-window-handle.blurb: Get a handle to the native window for an image display.
gimp-display-get-window-handle.help: This procedure returns a handle to the native window for a given image display. For example in the X backend of GDK, a native window handle is an Xlib XID. A value of 0 is returned for an invalid display or if this function is unimplemented for the windowing system that is being used.

args.name: display
args.desc: The display to get the window handle from

gimp-display-is-valid.blurb: Returns TRUE if the display is valid.
gimp-display-is-valid.help: This procedure checks if the given display ID is valid and refers to an existing display.

gimp-display-new.blurb: Create a new display for the specified image.
gimp-display-new.help: Creates a new display for the specified image. If the image already has a display, another is added. Multiple displays are handled transparently by GIMP. The newly created display is returned and can be subsequently destroyed with a call to 'gimp-display-delete'. This procedure only makes sense for use with the GIMP UI, and will result in an execution error if called when GIMP has no UI.

gimp-displays-flush.blurb: Flush all internal changes to the user interface
gimp-displays-flush.help: This procedure takes no arguments and returns nothing except a success status. Its purpose is to flush all pending updates of image manipulations to the user interface. It should be called whenever appropriate.

gimp-displays-reconnect.blurb: Reconnect displays from one image to another image.
gimp-displays-reconnect.help: This procedure connects all displays of the old_image to the new_image. If the old_image has no display or new_image already has a display the reconnect is not performed and the procedure returns without success. You should rarely need to use this function.

args.name: old-image
args.desc: The old image (must have at least one display)

args.name: new-image
args.desc: The new image (must not have a display)

args.name: exposure
args.desc: The exposure of the strokes (0 <= exposure <= 100)

args.name: dodgeburn-type
args.desc: The type either dodge or burn { DODGE-BURN-TYPE-DODGE (0), DODGE-BURN-TYPE-BURN (1) }

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-dodgeburn-default.blurb: Dodgeburn image with varying exposure. This is the same as the gimp_dodgeburn() function except that the exposure, type and mode are taken from the tools option dialog. If the dialog has not been activated then the defaults as used by the dialog will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-drawable-bpp.help: This procedure returns the number of bytes per pixel, which corresponds to the number of components unless 'gimp-plugin-enable-precision' was called.

gimp-drawable-brightness-contrast.blurb: Modify brightness/contrast in the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-brightness-contrast.help: This procedures allows the brightness and contrast of the specified drawable to be modified. Both 'brightness' and 'contrast' parameters are defined between -0.5 and 0.5.

gimp-drawable-bytes.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-drawable-bpp' instead.
gimp-drawable-bytes.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-drawable-bpp' instead.

gimp-drawable-color-balance.blurb: Modify the color balance of the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-color-balance.help: Modify the color balance of the specified drawable. There are three axis which can be modified: cyan-red, magenta-green, and yellow-blue. Negative values increase the amount of the former, positive values increase the amount of the latter. Color balance can be controlled with the 'transfer_mode' setting, which allows shadows, mid-tones, and highlights in an image to be affected differently. The 'preserve-lum' parameter, if TRUE, ensures that the luminosity of each pixel remains fixed.

args.name: preserve-lum
args.desc: Preserve luminosity values at each pixel (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-colorize-hsl.blurb: Render the drawable as a grayscale image seen through a colored glass.
gimp-drawable-colorize-hsl.help: Desaturates the drawable, then tints it with the specified color. This tool is only valid on RGB color images. It will not operate on grayscale drawables.

gimp-drawable-curves-explicit.blurb: Modifies the intensity curve(s) for specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-curves-explicit.help: Modifies the intensity mapping for one channel in the specified drawable. The channel can be either an intensity component, or the value. The 'values' parameter is an array of doubles which explicitly defines how each pixel value in the drawable will be modified. Use the 'gimp-curves-spline' function to modify intensity levels with Catmull Rom splines.

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: num-values
args.desc: The number of values in the new curve (256 <= num-values <= 2096)

gimp-drawable-curves-spline.blurb: Modifies the intensity curve(s) for specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-curves-spline.help: Modifies the intensity mapping for one channel in the specified drawable. The channel can be either an intensity component, or the value. The 'points' parameter is an array of doubles which define a set of control points which describe a Catmull Rom spline which yields the final intensity curve. Use the 'gimp-curves-explicit' function to explicitly modify intensity levels.

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: num-points
args.desc: The number of values in the control point array (4 <= num-points <= 2048)

args.name: points
args.desc: The spline control points: { cp1.x, cp1.y, cp2.x, cp2.y, ... }

gimp-drawable-delete.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.
gimp-drawable-delete.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.

gimp-drawable-desaturate.blurb: Desaturate the contents of the specified drawable, with the specified formula.
gimp-drawable-desaturate.help: This procedure desaturates the contents of the specified drawable, with the specified formula. This procedure only works on drawables of type RGB color.

args.name: desaturate-mode
args.desc: The formula to use to desaturate { DESATURATE-LIGHTNESS (0), DESATURATE-LUMA (1), DESATURATE-AVERAGE (2), DESATURATE-LUMINANCE (3), DESATURATE-VALUE (4) }

gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill.blurb: Fill the area by a seed fill starting at the specified coordinates.
gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill.help:
This procedure does a seed fill at the specified coordinates, using various parameters from the current context.
In the case of merged sampling, the x and y coordinates are relative to the image's origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-pattern', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-diagonal-neighbors', 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application.

gimp-drawable-edit-clear.help:
This procedure clears the specified drawable. If the drawable has an alpha channel, the cleared pixels will become transparent. If the drawable does not have an alpha channel, cleared pixels will be set to the background color. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-background'.

gimp-drawable-edit-fill.help:
This procedure fills the specified drawable according to fill mode. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active. If you want to fill the whole drawable, regardless of the selection, use 'gimp-drawable-fill'.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-pattern'.

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill.blurb: Draw a gradient between the starting and ending coordinates with the specified gradient type.
gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill.help:
This tool requires information on the gradient type. It creates the specified variety of gradient using the starting and ending coordinates as defined for each gradient type. For shapeburst gradient types, the context's distance metric is also relevant and can be updated with 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

args.name: gradient-type
args.desc: The type of gradient { GRADIENT-LINEAR (0), GRADIENT-BILINEAR (1), GRADIENT-RADIAL (2), GRADIENT-SQUARE (3), GRADIENT-CONICAL-SYMMETRIC (4), GRADIENT-CONICAL-ASYMMETRIC (5), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-ANGULAR (6), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-SPHERICAL (7), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-DIMPLED (8), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-CLOCKWISE (9), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-ANTICLOCKWISE (10) }

args.name: offset
args.desc: Offset relates to the starting and ending coordinates specified for the blend. This parameter is mode dependent. (offset >= 0)

args.name: supersample-max-depth
args.desc: Maximum recursion levels for supersampling (1 <= supersample-max-depth <= 9)

args.name: dither
args.desc: Use dithering to reduce banding (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x coordinate of this gradient's starting point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y coordinate of this gradient's starting point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x coordinate of this gradient's ending point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y coordinate of this gradient's ending point

gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item.help:
This procedure strokes the specified item, painting along its outline (e.g. along a path, or along a channel's boundary), with the active paint method and brush, or using a plain line with configurable properties.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-paint-method', 'gimp-context-set-stroke-method', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-brush' and all brush property settings, 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-line-width' and all line property settings, 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection.help:
This procedure strokes the current selection, painting along the selection boundary with the active paint method and brush, or using a plain line with configurable properties. The paint is applied to the specified drawable regardless of the active selection.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-paint-method', 'gimp-context-set-stroke-method', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-brush' and all brush property settings, 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-line-width' and all line property settings, 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

gimp-drawable-equalize.blurb: Equalize the contents of the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-equalize.help: This procedure equalizes the contents of the specified drawable. Each intensity channel is equalized independently. The equalized intensity is given as inten' = (255 - inten). The 'mask_only' option specifies whether to adjust only the area of the image within the selection bounds, or the entire image based on the histogram of the selected area. If there is no selection, the entire image is adjusted based on the histogram for the entire image.

gimp-drawable-fill.blurb: Fill the drawable with the specified fill mode.
gimp-drawable-fill.help:
This procedure fills the drawable. If the fill mode is foreground the current foreground color is used. If the fill mode is background, the current background color is used. If the fill type is white, then white is used. Transparent fill only affects layers with an alpha channel, in which case the alpha channel is set to transparent. If the drawable has no alpha channel, it is filled to white. No fill leaves the drawable's contents undefined.
This procedure is unlike 'gimp-edit-fill' or the bucket fill tool because it fills regardless of a selection. Its main purpose is to fill a newly created drawable before adding it to the image. This operation cannot be undone.

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

gimp-drawable-foreground-extract.blurb: Extract the foreground of a drawable using a given trimap.
gimp-drawable-foreground-extract.help: Image Segmentation by Uniform Color Clustering, see https://www.inf.fu-berlin.de/inst/pubs/tr-b-05-07.pdf

args.name: mode
args.desc: The algorithm to use { FOREGROUND-EXTRACT-SIOX (0), FOREGROUND-EXTRACT-MATTING (1) }

gimp-drawable-free-shadow.blurb: Free the specified drawable's shadow data (if it exists).
gimp-drawable-free-shadow.help: This procedure is intended as a memory saving device. If any shadow memory has been allocated, it will be freed automatically when the drawable is removed from the image, or when the plug-in procedure which allocated it returns.

gimp-drawable-get-format.help: This procedure returns the drawable's Babl format.

gimp-drawable-get-image.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.
gimp-drawable-get-image.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.

gimp-drawable-get-linked.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.
gimp-drawable-get-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.

gimp-drawable-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-drawable-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-drawable-get-pixel.blurb: Gets the value of the pixel at the specified coordinates.
gimp-drawable-get-pixel.help: This procedure gets the pixel value at the specified coordinates. The 'num_channels' argument must always be equal to the bytes-per-pixel value for the specified drawable.

gimp-drawable-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-drawable-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-drawable-get-thumbnail-format.blurb: Returns the drawable's thumbnail Babl format
gimp-drawable-get-thumbnail-format.help:
This procedure returns the drawable's thumbnail Babl format.
Thumbnails are always 8-bit images, see 'gimp-drawable-thumbnail' and 'gimp-drawable-sub-thmbnail'.

gimp-drawable-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-drawable-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-drawable-has-alpha.blurb: Returns TRUE if the drawable has an alpha channel.
gimp-drawable-has-alpha.help: This procedure returns whether the specified drawable has an alpha channel. This can only be true for layers, and the associated type will be one of: { RGBA , GRAYA, INDEXEDA }.

gimp-drawable-height.blurb: Returns the height of the drawable.
gimp-drawable-height.help: This procedure returns the specified drawable's height in pixels.

gimp-drawable-histogram.blurb: Returns information on the intensity histogram for the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-histogram.help: This tool makes it possible to gather information about the intensity histogram of a drawable. A channel to examine is first specified. This can be either value, red, green, or blue, depending on whether the drawable is of type color or grayscale. Second, a range of intensities are specified. The 'gimp-drawable-histogram' function returns statistics based on the pixels in the drawable that fall under this range of values. Mean, standard deviation, median, number of pixels, and percentile are all returned. Additionally, the total count of pixels in the image is returned. Counts of pixels are weighted by any associated alpha values and by the current selection mask. That is, pixels that lie outside an active selection mask will not be counted. Similarly, pixels with transparent alpha values will not be counted. The returned mean, std_dev and median are in the range (0..255) for 8-bit images or if the plug-in is not precision-aware, and in the range (0.0..1.0) otherwise.

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to query { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: start-range
args.desc: Start of the intensity measurement range (0 <= start-range <= 1)

args.name: end-range
args.desc: End of the intensity measurement range (0 <= end-range <= 1)

gimp-drawable-hue-saturation.blurb: Modify hue, lightness, and saturation in the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-hue-saturation.help: This procedure allows the hue, lightness, and saturation in the specified drawable to be modified. The 'hue-range' parameter provides the capability to limit range of affected hues. The 'overlap' parameter provides blending into neighboring hue channels when rendering.

args.name: hue-range
args.desc: Range of affected hues { HUE-RANGE-ALL (0), HUE-RANGE-RED (1), HUE-RANGE-YELLOW (2), HUE-RANGE-GREEN (3), HUE-RANGE-CYAN (4), HUE-RANGE-BLUE (5), HUE-RANGE-MAGENTA (6) }

gimp-drawable-invert.blurb: Invert the contents of the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-invert.help: This procedure inverts the contents of the specified drawable. Each intensity channel is inverted independently. The inverted intensity is given as inten' = (255 - inten). If 'linear' is TRUE, the drawable is inverted in linear space.

args.name: linear
args.desc: Whether to invert in linear space (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-is-channel.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-channel' instead.
gimp-drawable-is-channel.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-channel' instead.

gimp-drawable-is-gray.blurb: Returns whether the drawable is a grayscale type.
gimp-drawable-is-gray.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified drawable is of type { Gray, GrayA }.

gimp-drawable-is-indexed.blurb: Returns whether the drawable is an indexed type.
gimp-drawable-is-indexed.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified drawable is of type { Indexed, IndexedA }.

gimp-drawable-is-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-layer' instead.
gimp-drawable-is-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-layer' instead.

gimp-drawable-is-layer-mask.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-layer-mask' instead.
gimp-drawable-is-layer-mask.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-layer-mask' instead.

gimp-drawable-is-rgb.blurb: Returns whether the drawable is an RGB type.
gimp-drawable-is-rgb.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified drawable is of type { RGB, RGBA }.

gimp-drawable-is-text-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-text-layer' instead.
gimp-drawable-is-text-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-text-layer' instead.

gimp-drawable-is-valid.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.
gimp-drawable-is-valid.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.

gimp-drawable-levels.blurb: Modifies intensity levels in the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-levels.help: This tool allows intensity levels in the specified drawable to be remapped according to a set of parameters. The low/high input levels specify an initial mapping from the source intensities. The gamma value determines how intensities between the low and high input intensities are interpolated. A gamma value of 1.0 results in a linear interpolation. Higher gamma values result in more high-level intensities. Lower gamma values result in more low-level intensities. The low/high output levels constrain the final intensity mapping--that is, no final intensity will be lower than the low output level and no final intensity will be higher than the high output level. This tool is only valid on RGB color and grayscale images.

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: clamp-input
args.desc: Clamp input values before applying output levels (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: clamp-output
args.desc: Clamp final output values (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-levels-stretch.blurb: Automatically modifies intensity levels in the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-levels-stretch.help: This procedure allows intensity levels in the specified drawable to be remapped according to a set of guessed parameters. It is equivalent to clicking the "Auto" button in the Levels tool.

gimp-drawable-mask-bounds.blurb: Find the bounding box of the current selection in relation to the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-mask-bounds.help:
This procedure returns whether there is a selection. If there is one, the upper left and lower right-hand corners of its bounding box are returned. These coordinates are specified relative to the drawable's origin, and bounded by the drawable's extents. Please note that the pixel specified by the lower right-hand coordinate of the bounding box is not part of the selection. The selection ends at the upper left corner of this pixel. This means the width of the selection can be calculated as (x2 - x1), its height as (y2 - y1).
Note that the returned boolean does NOT correspond with the returned region being empty or not, it always returns whether the selection is non_empty. See 'gimp-drawable-mask-intersect' for a boolean return value which is more useful in most cases.

gimp-drawable-mask-intersect.blurb: Find the bounding box of the current selection in relation to the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-mask-intersect.help:
This procedure returns whether there is an intersection between the drawable and the selection. Unlike 'gimp-drawable-mask-bounds', the intersection's bounds are returned as x, y, width, height.
If there is no selection this function returns TRUE and the returned bounds are the extents of the whole drawable.

gimp-drawable-merge-shadow.blurb: Merge the shadow buffer with the specified drawable.
gimp-drawable-merge-shadow.help: This procedure combines the contents of the drawable's shadow buffer (for temporary processing) with the specified drawable. The 'undo' parameter specifies whether to add an undo step for the operation. Requesting no undo is useful for such applications as 'auto-apply'.

args.name: undo
args.desc: Push merge to undo stack? (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-offset.blurb: Offset the drawable by the specified amounts in the X and Y directions
gimp-drawable-offset.help: This procedure offsets the specified drawable by the amounts specified by 'offset_x' and 'offset_y'. If 'wrap_around' is set to TRUE, then portions of the drawable which are offset out of bounds are wrapped around. Alternatively, the undefined regions of the drawable can be filled with transparency or the background color, as specified by the 'fill-type' parameter.

args.name: wrap-around
args.desc: wrap image around or fill vacated regions (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: fill vacated regions of drawable with background or transparent { OFFSET-BACKGROUND (0), OFFSET-TRANSPARENT (1), OFFSET-WRAP-AROUND (2) }

args.name: offset-x
args.desc: offset by this amount in X direction

args.name: offset-y
args.desc: offset by this amount in Y direction

gimp-drawable-offsets.blurb: Returns the offsets for the drawable.
gimp-drawable-offsets.help: This procedure returns the specified drawable's offsets. This only makes sense if the drawable is a layer since channels are anchored. The offsets of a channel will be returned as 0.

gimp-drawable-parasite-attach.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-attach-parasite' instead.
gimp-drawable-parasite-attach.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-attach-parasite' instead.

args.name: parasite
args.desc: The parasite to attach to the item

gimp-drawable-posterize.help: This procedures reduces the number of shades allows in each intensity channel to the specified 'levels' parameter.

gimp-drawable-set-image.help: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-drawable-set-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-linked' instead.

args.desc: The new item linked state (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-set-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.
gimp-drawable-set-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.

gimp-drawable-set-pixel.blurb: Sets the value of the pixel at the specified coordinates.
gimp-drawable-set-pixel.help: This procedure sets the pixel value at the specified coordinates. The 'num_channels' argument must always be equal to the bytes-per-pixel value for the specified drawable. Note that this function is not undoable, you should use it only on drawables you just created yourself.

args.name: num-channels
args.desc: The number of channels for the pixel (num-channels >= 0)

gimp-drawable-set-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.
gimp-drawable-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-drawable-set-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-drawable-sub-thumbnail.blurb: Get a thumbnail of a sub-area of a drawable drawable.
gimp-drawable-sub-thumbnail.help: This function gets data from which a thumbnail of a drawable preview can be created. Maximum x or y dimension is 1024 pixels. The pixels are returned in RGB[A] or GRAY[A] format. The bpp return value gives the number of bytes in the image.

args.name: src-x
args.desc: The x coordinate of the area (src-x >= 0)

args.name: src-y
args.desc: The y coordinate of the area (src-y >= 0)

gimp-drawable-threshold.help: This procedures generates a threshold map of the specified drawable. All pixels between the values of 'low_threshold' and 'high_threshold', on the scale of 'channel' are replaced with white, and all other pixels with black.

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to base the threshold on { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

gimp-drawable-thumbnail.blurb: Get a thumbnail of a drawable.
gimp-drawable-thumbnail.help: This function gets data from which a thumbnail of a drawable preview can be created. Maximum x or y dimension is 1024 pixels. The pixels are returned in RGB[A] or GRAY[A] format. The bpp return value gives the number of bytes in the image.

gimp-drawable-type.help: This procedure returns the drawable's type.

gimp-drawable-type-with-alpha.blurb: Returns the drawable's type with alpha.
gimp-drawable-type-with-alpha.help: This procedure returns the drawable's type as if had an alpha channel. If the type is currently Gray, for instance, the returned type would be GrayA. If the drawable already has an alpha channel, the drawable's type is simply returned.

gimp-drawable-update.blurb: Update the specified region of the drawable.
gimp-drawable-update.help: This procedure updates the specified region of the drawable. The (x, y) coordinate pair is relative to the drawable's origin, not to the image origin. Therefore, the entire drawable can be updated using (0, 0, width, height).

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper left corner of update region

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper left corner of update region

gimp-drawable-width.blurb: Returns the width of the drawable.
gimp-drawable-width.help: This procedure returns the specified drawable's width in pixels.

gimp-dynamics-get-list.blurb: Retrieve the list of loaded paint dynamics.
gimp-dynamics-get-list.help: This procedure returns a list of the paint dynamics that are currently available.

args.name: filter
args.desc: An optional regular expression used to filter the list

gimp-dynamics-refresh.blurb: Refresh current paint dynamics. This function always succeeds.
gimp-dynamics-refresh.help: This procedure retrieves all paint dynamics currently in the user's paint dynamics path and updates the paint dynamics dialogs accordingly.

gimp-edit-blend.blurb: Blend between the starting and ending coordinates with the specified blend mode and gradient type.
gimp-edit-blend.help:
This tool requires information on the paint application mode, the blend mode, and the gradient type. It creates the specified variety of blend using the starting and ending coordinates as defined for each gradient type. For shapeburst gradient types, the context's distance metric is also relevant and can be updated with 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill' instead.

args.name: blend-mode
args.desc: The type of blend { BLEND-FG-BG-RGB (0), BLEND-FG-BG-HSV (1), BLEND-FG-TRANSPARENT (2), BLEND-CUSTOM (3) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.desc: Repeat mode { REPEAT-NONE (0), REPEAT-SAWTOOTH (1), REPEAT-TRIANGULAR (2), REPEAT-TRUNCATE (3) }

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: max-depth
args.desc: Maximum recursion levels for supersampling (1 <= max-depth <= 9)

args.name: dither
args.desc: Use dithering to reduce banding (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: y1

args.name: x2

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y coordinate of this blend's ending point

gimp-edit-bucket-fill.blurb: Fill the area specified either by the current selection if there is one, or by a seed fill starting at the specified coordinates.
gimp-edit-bucket-fill.help:
This tool requires information on the paint application mode, and the fill mode, which can either be in the foreground color, or in the currently active pattern. If there is no selection, a seed fill is executed at the specified coordinates and extends outward in keeping with the threshold parameter. If there is a selection in the target image, the threshold, sample merged, x, and y arguments are unused. If the sample_merged parameter is TRUE, the data of the composite image will be used instead of that for the specified drawable. This is equivalent to sampling for colors after merging all visible layers. In the case of merged sampling, the x and y coordinates are relative to the image's origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill' instead.

args.name: fill-mode
args.desc: The type of fill { BUCKET-FILL-FG (0), BUCKET-FILL-BG (1), BUCKET-FILL-PATTERN (2) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final bucket fill (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: The threshold determines how extensive the seed fill will be. It's value is specified in terms of intensity levels. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image. (0 <= threshold <= 255)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

gimp-edit-clear.help:
This procedure clears the specified drawable. If the drawable has an alpha channel, the cleared pixels will become transparent. If the drawable does not have an alpha channel, cleared pixels will be set to the background color. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-clear' instead.

gimp-edit-copy.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is copied from the specified drawable and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the specified drawable's contents will be stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer. This procedure will fail if the selected area lies completely outside the bounds of the current drawable and there is nothing to copy from.

gimp-edit-copy-visible.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is copied from the projection and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the projection's contents will be stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer.

gimp-edit-cut.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is cut from the specified drawable and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the specified drawable will be removed and its contents stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer. This procedure will fail if the selected area lies completely outside the bounds of the current drawable and there is nothing to copy from.

gimp-edit-fill.help:
This procedure fills the specified drawable with the fill mode. If the fill mode is foreground, the current foreground color is used. If the fill mode is background, the current background color is used. Other fill modes should not be used. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active. If you want to fill the whole drawable, regardless of the selection, use 'gimp-drawable-fill'.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-fill' instead.

args.name: fill-type
