args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

gimp-edit-named-copy.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-copy', but additionally stores the copied buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-copy-visible.blurb: Copy from the projection into a named buffer.
gimp-edit-named-copy-visible.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-copy-visible', but additionally stores the copied buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-cut.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-cut', but additionally stores the cut buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-paste.blurb: Paste named buffer to the specified drawable.
gimp-edit-named-paste.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-paste' but pastes a named buffer instead of the global buffer.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

args.name: paste-into
args.desc: Clear selection, or paste behind it? (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image' instead.
gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image' instead.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image.blurb: Paste named buffer to a new image.
gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' but pastes a named buffer instead of the global buffer.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

gimp-edit-paste.blurb: Paste buffer to the specified drawable.
gimp-edit-paste.help: This procedure pastes a copy of the internal GIMP edit buffer to the specified drawable. The GIMP edit buffer will be empty unless a call was previously made to either 'gimp-edit-cut' or 'gimp-edit-copy'. The "paste_into" option specifies whether to clear the current image selection, or to paste the buffer "behind" the selection. This allows the selection to act as a mask for the pasted buffer. Anywhere that the selection mask is non-zero, the pasted buffer will show through. The pasted buffer will be a new layer in the image which is designated as the image floating selection. If the image has a floating selection at the time of pasting, the old floating selection will be anchored to its drawable before the new floating selection is added. This procedure returns the new floating layer. The resulting floating selection will already be attached to the specified drawable, and a subsequent call to floating_sel_attach is not needed.

args.name: paste-into
args.desc: Clear selection, or paste behind it? (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-edit-paste-as-new.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' instead.
gimp-edit-paste-as-new.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' instead.

gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image.blurb: Paste buffer to a new image.
gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image.help: This procedure pastes a copy of the internal GIMP edit buffer to a new image. The GIMP edit buffer will be empty unless a call was previously made to either 'gimp-edit-cut' or 'gimp-edit-copy'. This procedure returns the new image or -1 if the edit buffer was empty.

gimp-edit-stroke.help:
This procedure strokes the current selection, painting along the selection boundary with the active brush and foreground color. The paint is applied to the specified drawable regardless of the active selection.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection' instead.

gimp-edit-stroke-vectors.help:
This procedure strokes the specified vectors object, painting along the path with the active brush and foreground color.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item' instead.

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-eraser.help: This tool erases using the current brush mask. If the specified drawable contains an alpha channel, then the erased pixels will become transparent. Otherwise, the eraser tool replaces the contents of the drawable with the background color. Like paintbrush, this tool linearly interpolates between the specified stroke coordinates.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: hardness
args.desc: How to apply the brush { BRUSH-HARD (0), BRUSH-SOFT (1) }

args.name: method
args.desc: The paint method to use { PAINT-CONSTANT (0), PAINT-INCREMENTAL (1) }

gimp-eraser-default.help: This tool erases using the current brush mask. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-eraser' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the eraser option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-file-load.blurb: Loads an image file by invoking the right load handler.
gimp-file-load.help: This procedure invokes the correct file load handler using magic if possible, and falling back on the file's extension and/or prefix if not. The name of the file to load is typically a full pathname, and the name entered is what the user actually typed before prepending a directory path. The reason for this is that if the user types https://www.gimp.org/foo.png he wants to fetch a URL, and the full pathname will not look like a URL.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The name as entered by the user

gimp-file-load-layer.blurb: Loads an image file as a layer for an existing image.
gimp-file-load-layer.help: This procedure behaves like the file-load procedure but opens the specified image as a layer for an existing image. The returned layer needs to be added to the existing image with 'gimp-image-insert-layer'.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

gimp-file-load-layers.blurb: Loads an image file as layers for an existing image.
gimp-file-load-layers.help: This procedure behaves like the file-load procedure but opens the specified image as layers for an existing image. The returned layers needs to be added to the existing image with 'gimp-image-insert-layer'.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load

gimp-file-load-thumbnail.blurb: Loads the thumbnail for a file.
gimp-file-load-thumbnail.help: This procedure tries to load a thumbnail that belongs to the file with the given filename. This name is a full pathname. The returned data is an array of colordepth 3 (RGB), regardless of the image type. Width and height of the thumbnail are also returned. Don't use this function if you need a thumbnail of an already opened image, use 'gimp-image-thumbnail' instead.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file that owns the thumbnail to load

gimp-file-save.help: This procedure invokes the correct file save handler according to the file's extension and/or prefix. The name of the file to save is typically a full pathname, and the name entered is what the user actually typed before prepending a directory path. The reason for this is that if the user types https://www.gimp.org/foo.png she wants to fetch a URL, and the full pathname will not look like a URL.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The name as entered by the user

gimp-file-save-thumbnail.blurb: Saves a thumbnail for the given image
gimp-file-save-thumbnail.help: This procedure saves a thumbnail for the given image according to the Free Desktop Thumbnail Managing Standard. The thumbnail is saved so that it belongs to the file with the given filename. This means you have to save the image under this name first, otherwise this procedure will fail. This procedure may become useful if you want to explicitly save a thumbnail with a file.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file the thumbnail belongs to

gimp-floating-sel-anchor.blurb: Anchor the specified floating selection to its associated drawable.
gimp-floating-sel-anchor.help: This procedure anchors the floating selection to its associated drawable. This is similar to merging with a merge type of ClipToBottomLayer. The floating selection layer is no longer valid after this operation.

gimp-floating-sel-attach.blurb: Attach the specified layer as floating to the specified drawable.
gimp-floating-sel-attach.help: This procedure attaches the layer as floating selection to the drawable.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer (is attached as floating selection)

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable (where to attach the floating selection)

gimp-floating-sel-relax.blurb: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.
gimp-floating-sel-relax.help: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-floating-sel-remove.blurb: Remove the specified floating selection from its associated drawable.
gimp-floating-sel-remove.help: This procedure removes the floating selection completely, without any side effects. The associated drawable is then set to active.

gimp-floating-sel-rigor.blurb: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.
gimp-floating-sel-rigor.help: Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-floating-sel-to-layer.blurb: Transforms the specified floating selection into a layer.
gimp-floating-sel-to-layer.help: This procedure transforms the specified floating selection into a layer with the same offsets and extents. The composited image will look precisely the same, but the floating selection layer will no longer be clipped to the extents of the drawable it was attached to. The floating selection will become the active layer. This procedure will not work if the floating selection has a different base type from the underlying image. This might be the case if the floating selection is above an auxiliary channel or a layer mask.

gimp-fonts-close-popup.help: This procedure closes an opened font selection dialog.

args.name: font-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this pop-up

gimp-fonts-get-list.blurb: Retrieve the list of loaded fonts.
gimp-fonts-get-list.help: This procedure returns a list of the fonts that are currently available.

args.name: filter
args.desc: An optional regular expression used to filter the list

gimp-fonts-popup.help: This procedure opens the font selection dialog.

args.name: font-callback
args.desc: The callback PDB proc to call when font selection is made

args.name: popup-title
args.desc: Title of the font selection dialog

args.name: initial-font
args.desc: The name of the font to set as the first selected

gimp-fonts-refresh.blurb: Refresh current fonts. This function always succeeds.
gimp-fonts-refresh.help: This procedure retrieves all fonts currently in the user's font path and updates the font dialogs accordingly. Depending on the amount of fonts on the system, this can take considerable time.

gimp-fonts-set-popup.blurb: Sets the current font in a font selection dialog.
gimp-fonts-set-popup.help: Sets the current font in a font selection dialog.

args.name: font-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this pop-up

args.name: font-name
args.desc: The name of the font to set as selected

args.name: num-segs
args.desc: Number of points (count 1 coordinate as two points) (num-segs >= 2)

args.name: segs
args.desc: Array of points: { p1.x, p1.y, p2.x, p2.y, ..., pn.x, pn.y}

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: feather-radius-x
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in X direction (feather-radius-x >= 0)

args.name: feather-radius-y
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in Y direction (feather-radius-y >= 0)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: select-transparent
args.desc: Whether to consider transparent pixels for selection. If TRUE, transparency is considered as a unique selectable color. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: select-criterion
args.desc: The criterion used to determine color similarity. SELECT_CRITERION_COMPOSITE is the standard choice. { SELECT-CRITERION-COMPOSITE (0), SELECT-CRITERION-R (1), SELECT-CRITERION-G (2), SELECT-CRITERION-B (3), SELECT-CRITERION-H (4), SELECT-CRITERION-S (5), SELECT-CRITERION-V (6), SELECT-CRITERION-A (7), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-L (8), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-C (9), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-H (10) }

gimp-get-color-configuration.blurb: Get a serialized version of the color management configuration.
gimp-get-color-configuration.help: Returns a string that can be deserialized into a GimpColorConfig object representing the current color management configuration.

gimp-get-default-comment.blurb: Get the default image comment as specified in the Preferences.
gimp-get-default-comment.help: Returns a copy of the default image comment.

gimp-get-default-unit.blurb: Get the default unit (taken from the user's locale).
gimp-get-default-unit.help: Returns the default unit's integer ID.

gimp-get-icon-theme-dir.blurb: Get the directory of the current icon theme.
gimp-get-icon-theme-dir.help:
Returns a copy of the current icon theme dir.

Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-get-module-load-inhibit.blurb: Get the list of modules which should not be loaded.
gimp-get-module-load-inhibit.help: Returns a copy of the list of modules which should not be loaded.

gimp-get-monitor-resolution.blurb: Get the monitor resolution as specified in the Preferences.
gimp-get-monitor-resolution.help: Returns the resolution of the monitor in pixels/inch. This value is taken from the Preferences (or the windowing system if this is set in the Preferences) and there's no guarantee for the value to be reasonable.

gimp-get-parasite.help: Finds and returns the global parasite that was previously attached.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-get-parasite-list.help: Returns a list of all currently attached global parasites.

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the required path.

gimp-get-theme-dir.blurb: Get the directory of the current GUI theme.
gimp-get-theme-dir.help:
Returns a copy of the current GUI theme dir.

Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-getpid.blurb: Returns the PID of the host GIMP process.
gimp-getpid.help: This procedure returns the process ID of the currently running GIMP.

gimp-gimprc-query.blurb: Queries the gimprc file parser for information on a specified token.
gimp-gimprc-query.help: This procedure is used to locate additional information contained in the gimprc file considered extraneous to the operation of GIMP. Plug-ins that need configuration information can expect it will be stored in the user gimprc file and can use this procedure to retrieve it. This query procedure will return the value associated with the specified token. This corresponds _only_ to entries with the format: (<token> <value>). The value must be a string. Entries not corresponding to this format will cause warnings to be issued on gimprc parsing and will not be queryable.

gimp-gimprc-set.blurb: Sets a gimprc token to a value and saves it in the gimprc.
gimp-gimprc-set.help: This procedure is used to add or change additional information in the gimprc file that is considered extraneous to the operation of GIMP. Plug-ins that need configuration information can use this function to store it, and 'gimp-gimprc-query' to retrieve it. This will accept _only_ string values in UTF-8 encoding.

args.name: token
args.desc: The token to add or modify

args.name: value
args.desc: The value to set the token to

gimp-gradient-duplicate.help: This procedure creates an identical gradient by a different name

gimp-gradient-get-custom-samples.blurb: Sample the specified gradient in custom positions.
gimp-gradient-get-custom-samples.help: This procedure samples the active gradient in the specified number of points. The procedure will sample the gradient in the specified positions from the list. The left endpoint of the gradient corresponds to position 0.0, and the right endpoint corresponds to 1.0. The procedure returns a list of floating-point values which correspond to the RGBA values for each sample.

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 1)

args.name: positions
args.desc: The list of positions to sample along the gradient

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-get-number-of-segments.blurb: Returns the number of segments of the specified gradient
gimp-gradient-get-number-of-segments.help: This procedure returns the number of segments of the specified gradient.

gimp-gradient-get-uniform-samples.blurb: Sample the specified in uniform parts.
gimp-gradient-get-uniform-samples.help: This procedure samples the active gradient in the specified number of uniform parts. It returns a list of floating-point values which correspond to the RGBA values for each sample. The minimum number of samples to take is 2, in which case the returned colors will correspond to the { 0.0, 1.0 } positions in the gradient. For example, if the number of samples is 3, the procedure will return the colors at positions { 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 }.

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 2)

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-is-editable.blurb: Tests if gradient can be edited
gimp-gradient-is-editable.help: Returns TRUE if you have permission to change the gradient

gimp-gradient-new.help: This procedure creates a new, uninitialized gradient

args.name: name
args.desc: The requested name of the new gradient

args.name: new-name
args.desc: The new name of the gradient

gimp-gradient-segment-get-blending-funct.blurb: Retrieves the gradient segment's blending function
gimp-gradient-segment-get-blending-funct.help: This procedure retrieves the blending function of the segment at the specified gradient name and segment index.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-coloring-type.blurb: Retrieves the gradient segment's coloring type
gimp-gradient-segment-get-coloring-type.help: This procedure retrieves the coloring type of the segment at the specified gradient name and segment index.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-color.blurb: Retrieves the left endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-color.help: This procedure retrieves the left endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-pos.blurb: Retrieves the left endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the left endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-middle-pos.blurb: Retrieves the middle point position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-middle-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the middle point position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-color.blurb: Retrieves the right endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-color.help: This procedure retrieves the right endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-pos.blurb: Retrieves the right endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the right endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-colors.blurb: Blend the colors of the segment range.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-colors.help: This function blends the colors (but not the opacity) of the segments' range of the gradient. Using it, the colors' transition will be uniform across the range.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-opacit.blurb: Blend the opacity of the segment range.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-opacit.help: This function blends the opacity (but not the colors) of the segments' range of the gradient. Using it, the opacity's transition will be uniform across the range.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-delete.help: This function deletes a segment range.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-flip.help: This function flips a segment range.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-move.blurb: Move the position of an entire segment range by a delta.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-move.help: This function moves the position of an entire segment range by a delta. The actual delta (which is returned) will be limited by the control points of the neighboring segments.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: delta
args.desc: The delta to move the segment range (-1 <= delta <= 1)

args.name: control-compress
args.desc: Whether or not to compress the neighboring segments (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-redistribute.blurb: Uniformly redistribute the segment range's handles
gimp-gradient-segment-range-redistribute.help: This function redistributes the handles of the specified segment range of the specified gradient, so they'll be evenly spaced.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-replicate.help: This function replicates a segment range a given number of times. Instead of the original segment range, several smaller scaled copies of it will appear in equal widths.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: replicate-times
args.desc: The number of times to replicate (2 <= replicate-times <= 20)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-blending.blurb: Change the blending function of a segments range
gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-blending.help: This function changes the blending function of a segment range to the specified blending function.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: blending-function
args.desc: The blending function { GRADIENT-SEGMENT-LINEAR (0), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-CURVED (1), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SINE (2), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SPHERE-INCREASING (3), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SPHERE-DECREASING (4), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-STEP (5) }

gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-coloring.blurb: Change the coloring type of a segments range
gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-coloring.help: This function changes the coloring type of a segment range to the specified coloring type.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: coloring-type
args.desc: The coloring type { GRADIENT-SEGMENT-RGB (0), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-HSV-CCW (1), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-HSV-CW (2) }

gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-midpoi.blurb: Splits each segment in the segment range at midpoint
gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-midpoi.help: This function splits each segment in the segment range at its midpoint.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-unifor.blurb: Splits each segment in the segment range uniformly
gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-unifor.help: This function splits each segment in the segment range uniformly according to the number of times specified by the parameter.

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: split-parts
args.desc: The number of uniform divisions to split each segment to (2 <= split-parts <= 1024)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-color.blurb: Sets the left endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-color.help: This procedure sets the left endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity to set for the endpoint (0 <= opacity <= 100)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-pos.blurb: Sets the left endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-pos.help:
This procedure sets the left endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the position of the middle point to the left to the middle point of the current segment.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-middle-pos.blurb: Sets the middle point position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-middle-pos.help:
This procedure sets the middle point position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the two endpoints of the segment.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-color.blurb: Sets the right endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-color.help: This procedure sets the right endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity to set for the endpoint (0 <= opacity <= 100)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-pos.blurb: Sets the right endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-pos.help:
This procedure sets the right endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the position of the middle point of the current segment and the middle point of the segment to the right.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-gradients-close-popup.help: This procedure closes an opened gradient selection dialog.

args.name: gradient-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this pop-up

gimp-gradients-get-active.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-get-gradient' instead.
gimp-gradients-get-active.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-get-gradient' instead.

gimp-gradients-get-gradient.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-get-gradient' instead.
gimp-gradients-get-gradient.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-get-gradient' instead.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name ("" means current active gradient)

args.name: sample-size
args.desc: Size of the sample to return when the gradient is changed (1 <= sample-size <= 10000)

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradients-get-list.blurb: Retrieve the list of loaded gradients.
gimp-gradients-get-list.help: This procedure returns a list of the gradients that are currently loaded. You can later use the 'gimp-context-set-gradient' function to set the active gradient.

args.name: filter
args.desc: An optional regular expression used to filter the list

gimp-gradients-popup.help: This procedure opens the gradient selection dialog.

args.name: gradient-callback
args.desc: The callback PDB proc to call when gradient selection is made

args.name: popup-title
args.desc: Title of the gradient selection dialog

args.name: initial-gradient
args.desc: The name of the gradient to set as the first selected

args.name: sample-size
args.desc: Size of the sample to return when the gradient is changed (1 <= sample-size <= 10000)

gimp-gradients-refresh.blurb: Refresh current gradients. This function always succeeds.
gimp-gradients-refresh.help: This procedure retrieves all gradients currently in the user's gradient path and updates the gradient dialogs accordingly.

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 0)

args.name: positions
args.desc: The list of positions to sample along the gradient

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 2)

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradients-set-active.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-set-gradient' instead.
gimp-gradients-set-active.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-set-gradient' instead.

gimp-gradients-set-gradient.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-set-gradient' instead.
gimp-gradients-set-gradient.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-context-set-gradient' instead.

gimp-gradients-set-popup.blurb: Sets the current gradient in a gradient selection dialog.
gimp-gradients-set-popup.help: Sets the current gradient in a gradient selection dialog.

args.name: gradient-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this pop-up

args.name: gradient-name
args.desc: The name of the gradient to set as selected

gimp-heal.blurb: Heal from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-heal.help: This tool heals the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. For image healing, if the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets exceeds the extents of the src drawable, then no paint is transferred. The healing tool is capable of transforming between any image types except RGB->Indexed.

args.name: src-x
args.desc: The x coordinate in the source image

args.name: src-y
args.desc: The y coordinate in the source image

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-heal-default.blurb: Heal from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-heal-default.help: This tool heals from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-heal' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the healing option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-help.help: This procedure loads the specified help page into the helpbrowser or what ever is configured as help viewer. The help page is identified by its domain and ID: if help_domain is NULL, we use the help_domain which was registered using the 'gimp-plugin-help-register' procedure. If help_domain is NULL and no help domain was registered, the help domain of the main GIMP installation is used.

args.name: help-domain
args.desc: The help domain in which help_id is registered

args.name: channel
args.desc: The channel to modify { HISTOGRAM-VALUE (0), HISTOGRAM-RED (1), HISTOGRAM-GREEN (2), HISTOGRAM-BLUE (3), HISTOGRAM-ALPHA (4), HISTOGRAM-LUMINANCE (5), HISTOGRAM-RGB (6) }

args.name: start-range
args.desc: Start of the intensity measurement range (0 <= start-range <= 255)

args.name: end-range
args.desc: End of the intensity measurement range (0 <= end-range <= 255)

args.name: hue-range
args.desc: Range of affected hues { HUE-RANGE-ALL (0), HUE-RANGE-RED (1), HUE-RANGE-YELLOW (2), HUE-RANGE-GREEN (3), HUE-RANGE-CYAN (4), HUE-RANGE-BLUE (5), HUE-RANGE-MAGENTA (6) }

gimp-image-active-drawable.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-active-drawable' instead.
gimp-image-active-drawable.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-active-drawable' instead.

gimp-image-add-hguide.blurb: Add a horizontal guide to an image.
gimp-image-add-hguide.help: This procedure adds a horizontal guide to an image. It takes the input image and the y-position of the new guide as parameters. It returns the guide ID of the new guide.

args.name: yposition
args.desc: The guide's y-offset from top of image (yposition >= 0)

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to receive the mask

args.name: mask
args.desc: The mask to add to the layer

gimp-image-add-sample-point.blurb: Add a sample point to an image.
gimp-image-add-sample-point.help: This procedure adds a sample point to an image. It takes the input image and the position of the new sample points as parameters. It returns the sample point ID of the new sample point.

args.name: position-x
args.desc: The guide'sample points x-offset from left of image (position-x >= 0)

args.name: position-y
args.desc: The guide'sample points y-offset from top of image (position-y >= 0)

gimp-image-add-vguide.blurb: Add a vertical guide to an image.
gimp-image-add-vguide.help: This procedure adds a vertical guide to an image. It takes the input image and the x-position of the new guide as parameters. It returns the guide ID of the new guide.

args.name: xposition
args.desc: The guide's x-offset from left of image (xposition >= 0)

gimp-image-attach-parasite.blurb: Add a parasite to an image.
gimp-image-attach-parasite.help: This procedure attaches a parasite to an image. It has no return values.

args.name: parasite
args.desc: The parasite to attach to an image

gimp-image-base-type.blurb: Get the base type of the image.
gimp-image-base-type.help: This procedure returns the image's base type. Layers in the image must be of this subtype, but can have an optional alpha channel.

gimp-image-clean-all.blurb: Set the image dirty count to 0.
gimp-image-clean-all.help: This procedure sets the specified image's dirty count to 0, allowing operations to occur without having a 'dirtied' image. This is especially useful for creating and loading images which should not initially be considered dirty, even though layers must be created, filled, and installed in the image. Note that save plug-ins must NOT call this function themselves after saving the image.

gimp-image-convert-color-profile.blurb: Convert the image's layers to a color profile
gimp-image-convert-color-profile.help: This procedure converts from the image's color profile (or the default RGB or grayscale profile if none is set) to the given color profile. Only RGB and grayscale color profiles are accepted, according to the image's type.

args.name: num-bytes
args.desc: Number of bytes in the color_profile array (num-bytes >= 0)

gimp-image-convert-color-profile-from-fi.blurb: Convert the image's layers to a color profile
gimp-image-convert-color-profile-from-fi.help: This procedure converts from the image's color profile (or the default RGB or grayscale profile if none is set) to an ICC profile specified by 'uri'. Only RGB and grayscale color profiles are accepted, according to the image's type.

args.name: uri
args.desc: The URI of the file containing the new color profile

gimp-image-convert-grayscale.help: This procedure converts the specified image to grayscale. This process requires an image in RGB or Indexed color mode.

gimp-image-convert-indexed.blurb: Convert specified image to and Indexed image
gimp-image-convert-indexed.help: This procedure converts the specified image to 'indexed' color. This process requires an image in RGB or Grayscale mode. The 'palette_type' specifies what kind of palette to use, A type of '0' means to use an optimal palette of 'num_cols' generated from the colors in the image. A type of '1' means to re-use the previous palette (not currently implemented). A type of '2' means to use the so-called WWW-optimized palette. Type '3' means to use only black and white colors. A type of '4' means to use a palette from the gimp palettes directories. The 'dither type' specifies what kind of dithering to use. '0' means no dithering, '1' means standard Floyd-Steinberg error diffusion, '2' means Floyd-Steinberg error diffusion with reduced bleeding, '3' means dithering based on pixel location ('Fixed' dithering).

args.name: dither-type
args.desc: The dither type to use { CONVERT-DITHER-NONE (0), CONVERT-DITHER-FS (1), CONVERT-DITHER-FS-LOWBLEED (2), CONVERT-DITHER-FIXED (3) }

args.name: palette-type
args.desc: The type of palette to use { CONVERT-PALETTE-GENERATE (0), CONVERT-PALETTE-WEB (2), CONVERT-PALETTE-MONO (3), CONVERT-PALETTE-CUSTOM (4) }

args.name: num-cols
args.desc: The number of colors to quantize to, ignored unless (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_GENERATE)

args.name: alpha-dither
args.desc: Dither transparency to fake partial opacity (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: remove-unused
args.desc: Remove unused or duplicate color entries from final palette, ignored if (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_GENERATE) (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: palette
args.desc: The name of the custom palette to use, ignored unless (palette_type == GIMP_CONVERT_PALETTE_CUSTOM)

gimp-image-convert-precision.blurb: Convert the image to the specified precision
gimp-image-convert-precision.help: This procedure converts the image to the specified precision. Note that indexed images cannot be converted and are always in GIMP_PRECISION_U8.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The new precision { PRECISION-U8-LINEAR (100), PRECISION-U8-GAMMA (150), PRECISION-U16-LINEAR (200), PRECISION-U16-GAMMA (250), PRECISION-U32-LINEAR (300), PRECISION-U32-GAMMA (350), PRECISION-HALF-LINEAR (500), PRECISION-HALF-GAMMA (550), PRECISION-FLOAT-LINEAR (600), PRECISION-FLOAT-GAMMA (650), PRECISION-DOUBLE-LINEAR (700), PRECISION-DOUBLE-GAMMA (750) }

gimp-image-convert-rgb.blurb: Convert specified image to RGB color
gimp-image-convert-rgb.help: This procedure converts the specified image to RGB color. This process requires an image in Grayscale or Indexed color mode. No image content is lost in this process aside from the colormap for an indexed image.

gimp-image-convert-set-dither-matrix.blurb: Set dither matrix for conversion to indexed
gimp-image-convert-set-dither-matrix.help: This procedure sets the dither matrix used when converting images to INDEXED mode with positional dithering.

args.name: width
args.desc: Width of the matrix (0 to reset to default matrix)

args.name: height
args.desc: Height of the matrix (0 to reset to default matrix)

args.name: matrix
args.desc: The matrix -- all values must be >= 1

gimp-image-crop.blurb: Crop the image to the specified extents.
gimp-image-crop.help: This procedure crops the image so that it's new width and height are equal to the supplied parameters. Offsets are also provided which describe the position of the previous image's content. All channels and layers within the image are cropped to the new image extents; this includes the image selection mask. If any parameters are out of range, an error is returned.

args.name: new-width
args.desc: New image width: (0 < new_width <= width) (1 <= new-width <= 524288)

args.name: new-height
args.desc: New image height: (0 < new_height <= height) (1 <= new-height <= 524288)

gimp-image-delete.help: If there are no displays associated with this image it will be deleted. This means that you can not delete an image through the PDB that was created by the user. If the associated display was however created through the PDB and you know the display ID, you may delete the display. Removal of the last associated display will then delete the image.

gimp-image-delete-guide.blurb: Deletes a guide from an image.
gimp-image-delete-guide.help: This procedure takes an image and a guide ID as input and removes the specified guide from the specified image.

args.name: guide
args.desc: The ID of the guide to be removed

gimp-image-delete-sample-point.blurb: Deletes a sample point from an image.
gimp-image-delete-sample-point.help: This procedure takes an image and a sample point ID as input and removes the specified sample point from the specified image.

args.name: sample-point
args.desc: The ID of the sample point to be removed

gimp-image-detach-parasite.blurb: Removes a parasite from an image.
gimp-image-detach-parasite.help: This procedure detaches a parasite from an image. It has no return values.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach from an image.

gimp-image-duplicate.help: This procedure duplicates the specified image, copying all layers, channels, and image information.

gimp-image-find-next-guide.blurb: Find next guide on an image.
gimp-image-find-next-guide.help: This procedure takes an image and a guide ID as input and finds the guide ID of the successor of the given guide ID in the image's guide list. If the supplied guide ID is 0, the procedure will return the first Guide. The procedure will return 0 if given the final guide ID as an argument or the image has no guides.

args.name: guide
args.desc: The ID of the current guide (0 if first invocation)

gimp-image-find-next-sample-point.blurb: Find next sample point on an image.
gimp-image-find-next-sample-point.help: This procedure takes an image and a sample point ID as input and finds the sample point ID of the successor of the given sample point ID in the image's sample point list. If the supplied sample point ID is 0, the procedure will return the first sample point. The procedure will return 0 if given the final sample point ID as an argument or the image has no sample points.

args.name: sample-point
args.desc: The ID of the current sample point (0 if first invocation)

gimp-image-flatten.blurb: Flatten all visible layers into a single layer. Discard all invisible layers.
gimp-image-flatten.help: This procedure combines the visible layers in a manner analogous to merging with the CLIP_TO_IMAGE merge type. Non-visible layers are discarded, and the resulting image is stripped of its alpha channel.

gimp-image-flip.blurb: Flips the image horizontally or vertically.

gimp-image-floating-sel-attached-to.blurb: Return the drawable the floating selection is attached to.
gimp-image-floating-sel-attached-to.help: This procedure returns the drawable the image's floating selection is attached to, if it exists. If it doesn't exist, -1 is returned as the drawable ID.

gimp-image-floating-selection.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-floating-sel' instead.
gimp-image-floating-selection.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-floating-sel' instead.

gimp-image-freeze-channels.help:
This procedure freezes the channel list of the image, suppressing any updates to the Channels dialog in response to changes to the image's channels. This can significantly improve performance while applying changes affecting the channel list.

Each call to 'gimp-image-freeze-channels' should be matched by a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-thaw-channels', undoing its effects.

gimp-image-freeze-layers.help:
This procedure freezes the layer list of the image, suppressing any updates to the Layers dialog in response to changes to the image's layers. This can significantly improve performance while applying changes affecting the layer list.

Each call to 'gimp-image-freeze-layers' should be matched by a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-thaw-layers', undoing its effects.

gimp-image-freeze-vectors.help:
This procedure freezes the vectors list of the image, suppressing any updates to the Paths dialog in response to changes to the image's vectors. This can significantly improve performance while applying changes affecting the vectors list.

Each call to 'gimp-image-freeze-vectors' should be matched by a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-thaw-vectors', undoing its effects.

gimp-image-get-active-channel.blurb: Returns the specified image's active channel.
gimp-image-get-active-channel.help: If there is an active channel, this will return the channel ID, otherwise, -1.

gimp-image-get-active-drawable.help: This procedure returns the ID of the image's active drawable. This can be either a layer, a channel, or a layer mask. The active drawable is specified by the active image channel. If that is -1, then by the active image layer. If the active image layer has a layer mask and the layer mask is in edit mode, then the layer mask is the active drawable.

gimp-image-get-active-layer.blurb: Returns the specified image's active layer.
gimp-image-get-active-layer.help: If there is an active layer, its ID will be returned, otherwise, -1. If a channel is currently active, then no layer will be. If a layer mask is active, then this will return the associated layer.

gimp-image-get-active-vectors.blurb: Returns the specified image's active vectors.
gimp-image-get-active-vectors.help: If there is an active path, its ID will be returned, otherwise, -1.

gimp-image-get-channel-by-name.blurb: Find a channel with a given name in an image.
gimp-image-get-channel-by-name.help: This procedure returns the channel with the given name in the specified image.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the channel to find

gimp-image-get-channel-by-tattoo.blurb: Find a channel with a given tattoo in an image.
gimp-image-get-channel-by-tattoo.help: This procedure returns the channel with the given tattoo in the specified image.

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the channel to find

gimp-image-get-channel-position.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.
gimp-image-get-channel-position.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.

gimp-image-get-channels.blurb: Returns the list of channels contained in the specified image.
gimp-image-get-channels.help: This procedure returns the list of channels contained in the specified image. This does not include the selection mask, or layer masks. The order is from topmost to bottommost. Note that "channels" are custom channels and do not include the image's color components.

gimp-image-get-cmap.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-colormap' instead.
gimp-image-get-cmap.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-colormap' instead.

gimp-image-get-color-profile.help: This procedure returns the image's color profile, or NULL if the image has no color profile assigned.

gimp-image-get-colormap.help: This procedure returns an actual pointer to the image's colormap, as well as the number of bytes contained in the colormap. The actual number of colors in the transmitted colormap will be 'num-bytes' / 3. If the image is not in Indexed color mode, no colormap is returned.

gimp-image-get-component-active.blurb: Returns if the specified image's image component is active.
gimp-image-get-component-active.help: This procedure returns if the specified image's image component (i.e. Red, Green, Blue intensity channels in an RGB image) is active or inactive -- whether or not it can be modified. If the specified component is not valid for the image type, an error is returned.

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }

gimp-image-get-component-visible.blurb: Returns if the specified image's image component is visible.
gimp-image-get-component-visible.help: This procedure returns if the specified image's image component (i.e. Red, Green, Blue intensity channels in an RGB image) is visible or invisible -- whether or not it can be seen. If the specified component is not valid for the image type, an error is returned.

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }

gimp-image-get-default-new-layer-mode.blurb: Get the default mode for newly created layers of this image.
gimp-image-get-default-new-layer-mode.help: Returns the default mode for newly created layers of this image.

gimp-image-get-effective-color-profile.blurb: Returns the color profile that is used for the image
gimp-image-get-effective-color-profile.help: This procedure returns the color profile that is actually used for this image, which is the profile returned by 'gimp-image-get-color-profile' if the image has a profile assigned, or a generated default RGB or grayscale profile, according to the image's type.

gimp-image-get-exported-uri.blurb: Returns the exported URI for the specified image.
gimp-image-get-exported-uri.help: This procedure returns the URI associated with the specified image if the image was exported a non-native GIMP format. If the image was not exported, this procedure returns %NULL.

gimp-image-get-filename.help: This procedure returns the specified image's filename in the filesystem encoding. The image has a filename only if it was loaded or imported from a file or has since been saved or exported. Otherwise, this function returns %NULL. See also 'gimp-image-get-uri'.

gimp-image-get-floating-sel.blurb: Return the floating selection of the image.
gimp-image-get-floating-sel.help: This procedure returns the image's floating selection, if it exists. If it doesn't exist, -1 is returned as the layer ID.

gimp-image-get-guide-orientation.blurb: Get orientation of a guide on an image.
gimp-image-get-guide-orientation.help: This procedure takes an image and a guide ID as input and returns the orientations of the guide.

gimp-image-get-guide-position.blurb: Get position of a guide on an image.
gimp-image-get-guide-position.help: This procedure takes an image and a guide ID as input and returns the position of the guide relative to the top or left of the image.

gimp-image-get-imported-uri.blurb: Returns the imported URI for the specified image.
gimp-image-get-imported-uri.help: This procedure returns the URI associated with the specified image if the image was imported from a non-native Gimp format. If the image was not imported, or has since been saved in the native Gimp format, this procedure returns %NULL.

gimp-image-get-item-position.blurb: Returns the position of the item in its level of its item tree.
gimp-image-get-item-position.help: This procedure determines the position of the specified item in its level in its item tree in the image. If the item doesn't exist in the image, or the item is not part of an item tree, an error is returned.

gimp-image-get-layer-by-name.blurb: Find a layer with a given name in an image.
gimp-image-get-layer-by-name.help: This procedure returns the layer with the given name in the specified image.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the layer to find

gimp-image-get-layer-by-tattoo.blurb: Find a layer with a given tattoo in an image.
gimp-image-get-layer-by-tattoo.help: This procedure returns the layer with the given tattoo in the specified image.

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the layer to find

gimp-image-get-layer-position.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.
gimp-image-get-layer-position.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.

gimp-image-get-layers.blurb: Returns the list of layers contained in the specified image.
gimp-image-get-layers.help: This procedure returns the list of layers contained in the specified image. The order of layers is from topmost to bottommost.

gimp-image-get-metadata.help: Returns exif/iptc/xmp metadata from the image.

gimp-image-get-name.help: This procedure returns the image's name. If the image has a filename or an URI, then the returned name contains the filename's or URI's base name (the last component of the path). Otherwise it is the translated string "Untitled". The returned name is formatted like the image name in the image window title, it may contain '[]', '(imported)' etc. and should only be used to label user interface elements. Never use it to construct filenames.

gimp-image-get-parasite.blurb: Look up a parasite in an image
gimp-image-get-parasite.help: Finds and returns the parasite that was previously attached to an image.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-image-get-parasite-list.help: Returns a list of all currently attached parasites.

gimp-image-get-precision.blurb: Get the precision of the image.
gimp-image-get-precision.help: This procedure returns the image's precision.

gimp-image-get-resolution.help: This procedure returns the specified image's resolution in dots per inch. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image.

gimp-image-get-sample-point-position.blurb: Get position of a sample point on an image.
gimp-image-get-sample-point-position.help: This procedure takes an image and a sample point ID as input and returns the position of the sample point relative to the top and left of the image.

gimp-image-get-selection.help: This will always return a valid ID for a selection -- which is represented as a channel internally.

gimp-image-get-tattoo-state.blurb: Returns the tattoo state associated with the image.
gimp-image-get-tattoo-state.help: This procedure returns the tattoo state of the image. Use only by save/load plug-ins that wish to preserve an images tattoo state. Using this function at other times will produce unexpected results.

gimp-image-get-unit.help: This procedure returns the specified image's unit. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image. See the gimp_unit_*() procedure definitions for the valid range of unit IDs and a description of the unit system.

gimp-image-get-uri.blurb: Returns the URI for the specified image.
gimp-image-get-uri.help: This procedure returns the URI associated with the specified image. The image has an URI only if it was loaded or imported from a file or has since been saved or exported. Otherwise, this function returns %NULL. See also gimp-image-get-imported-uri to get the URI of the current file if it was imported from a non-GIMP file format and not yet saved, or gimp-image-get-exported-uri if the image has been exported to a non-GIMP file format.

gimp-image-get-vectors.blurb: Returns the list of vectors contained in the specified image.
gimp-image-get-vectors.help: This procedure returns the list of vectors contained in the specified image.

gimp-image-get-vectors-by-name.blurb: Find a vectors with a given name in an image.
gimp-image-get-vectors-by-name.help: This procedure returns the vectors with the given name in the specified image.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the vectors to find

gimp-image-get-vectors-by-tattoo.blurb: Find a vectors with a given tattoo in an image.
gimp-image-get-vectors-by-tattoo.help: This procedure returns the vectors with the given tattoo in the specified image.

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the vectors to find

gimp-image-get-vectors-position.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.
gimp-image-get-vectors-position.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.

gimp-image-get-xcf-uri.blurb: Returns the XCF URI for the specified image.
gimp-image-get-xcf-uri.help: This procedure returns the XCF URI associated with the image. If there is no such URI, this procedure returns %NULL.

gimp-image-grid-get-background-color.blurb: Sets the background color of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-get-background-color.help: This procedure gets the background color of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-get-foreground-color.blurb: Sets the foreground color of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-get-foreground-color.help: This procedure gets the foreground color of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-get-offset.blurb: Gets the offset of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-get-offset.help: This procedure retrieves the horizontal and vertical offset of an image's grid. It takes the image as parameter.

gimp-image-grid-get-spacing.blurb: Gets the spacing of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-get-spacing.help: This procedure retrieves the horizontal and vertical spacing of an image's grid. It takes the image as parameter.

gimp-image-grid-get-style.blurb: Gets the style of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-get-style.help: This procedure retrieves the style of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-set-background-color.blurb: Gets the background color of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-set-background-color.help: This procedure sets the background color of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-set-foreground-color.blurb: Gets the foreground color of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-set-foreground-color.help: This procedure sets the foreground color of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-set-offset.blurb: Sets the offset of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-set-offset.help: This procedure sets the horizontal and vertical offset of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-set-spacing.blurb: Sets the spacing of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-set-spacing.help: This procedure sets the horizontal and vertical spacing of an image's grid.

gimp-image-grid-set-style.blurb: Sets the style unit of an image's grid.
gimp-image-grid-set-style.help: This procedure sets the style of an image's grid. It takes the image and the new style as parameters.

args.name: style
args.desc: The image's grid style { GRID-DOTS (0), GRID-INTERSECTIONS (1), GRID-ON-OFF-DASH (2), GRID-DOUBLE-DASH (3), GRID-SOLID (4) }

gimp-image-height.blurb: Return the height of the image
gimp-image-height.help: This procedure returns the image's height. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image. This is the "canvas" height.

gimp-image-insert-channel.blurb: Add the specified channel to the image.
gimp-image-insert-channel.help: This procedure adds the specified channel to the image at the given position. Since channel groups are not currently supported, the parent argument must always be 0. The position argument specifies the location of the channel inside the stack, starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1, then the channel is inserted above the active channel.

gimp-image-insert-layer.blurb: Add the specified layer to the image.
gimp-image-insert-layer.help: This procedure adds the specified layer to the image at the given position. If the specified parent is a valid layer group (See 'gimp-item-is-group' and 'gimp-layer-group-new') then the layer is added inside the group. If the parent is 0, the layer is added inside the main stack, outside of any group. The position argument specifies the location of the layer inside the stack (or the group, if a valid parent was supplied), starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1 and the parent is specified as 0, then the layer is inserted above the active layer, or inside the group if the active layer is a layer group. The layer type must be compatible with the image base type.

gimp-image-insert-vectors.blurb: Add the specified vectors to the image.
gimp-image-insert-vectors.help: This procedure adds the specified vectors to the image at the given position. Since vectors groups are not currently supported, the parent argument must always be 0. The position argument specifies the location of the vectors inside the stack, starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1, then the vectors is inserted above the active vectors.

gimp-image-is-dirty.blurb: Checks if the image has unsaved changes.
gimp-image-is-dirty.help: This procedure checks the specified image's dirty count to see if it needs to be saved. Note that saving the image does not automatically set the dirty count to 0, you need to call 'gimp-image-clean-all' after calling a save procedure to make the image clean.

gimp-image-is-valid.blurb: Returns TRUE if the image is valid.
gimp-image-is-valid.help: This procedure checks if the given image ID is valid and refers to an existing image.

gimp-image-list.blurb: Returns the list of images currently open.
gimp-image-list.help: This procedure returns the list of images currently open in GIMP.

gimp-image-lower-channel.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.
gimp-image-lower-channel.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.

gimp-image-lower-item.blurb: Lower the specified item in its level in its item tree
gimp-image-lower-item.help: This procedure lowers the specified item one step in the item tree. The procedure call will fail if there is no item below it.

gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom.blurb: Lower the specified item to the bottom of its level in its item tree
gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom.help: This procedure lowers the specified item to bottom of its level in the item tree. It will not move the layer if there is no layer below it.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to lower to bottom

gimp-image-lower-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.
gimp-image-lower-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.

gimp-image-lower-layer-to-bottom.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.
gimp-image-lower-layer-to-bottom.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to lower to bottom

gimp-image-lower-vectors.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.
gimp-image-lower-vectors.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.

gimp-image-lower-vectors-to-bottom.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.
gimp-image-lower-vectors-to-bottom.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to lower to bottom

gimp-image-merge-down.blurb: Merge the layer passed and the first visible layer below.
gimp-image-merge-down.help: This procedure combines the passed layer and the first visible layer below it using the specified merge type. A merge type of EXPAND_AS_NECESSARY expands the final layer to encompass the areas of the visible layers. A merge type of CLIP_TO_IMAGE clips the final layer to the extents of the image. A merge type of CLIP_TO_BOTTOM_LAYER clips the final layer to the size of the bottommost layer.

args.name: merge-layer
args.desc: The layer to merge down from

args.name: merge-type
args.desc: The type of merge { EXPAND-AS-NECESSARY (0), CLIP-TO-IMAGE (1), CLIP-TO-BOTTOM-LAYER (2) }

gimp-image-merge-layer-group.blurb: Merge the passed layer group's layers into one normal layer.
gimp-image-merge-layer-group.help: This procedure combines the layers of the passed layer group into a single normal layer, replacing the group.

gimp-image-merge-visible-layers.blurb: Merge the visible image layers into one.
gimp-image-merge-visible-layers.help: This procedure combines the visible layers into a single layer using the specified merge type. A merge type of EXPAND_AS_NECESSARY expands the final layer to encompass the areas of the visible layers. A merge type of CLIP_TO_IMAGE clips the final layer to the extents of the image. A merge type of CLIP_TO_BOTTOM_LAYER clips the final layer to the size of the bottommost layer.

args.name: merge-type
args.desc: The type of merge { EXPAND-AS-NECESSARY (0), CLIP-TO-IMAGE (1), CLIP-TO-BOTTOM-LAYER (2) }

gimp-image-new.blurb: Creates a new image with the specified width, height, and type.
gimp-image-new.help:
Creates a new image, undisplayed, with the specified extents and type. A layer should be created and added before this image is displayed, or subsequent calls to 'gimp-display-new' with this image as an argument will fail. Layers can be created using the 'gimp-layer-new' commands. They can be added to an image using the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command.

If your image's type if INDEXED, a colormap must also be added with 'gimp-image-set-colormap'. An indexed image without a colormap will output unexpected colors.

args.name: width
args.desc: The width of the image (1 <= width <= 524288)

args.name: height
args.desc: The height of the image (1 <= height <= 524288)

args.name: type
args.desc: The type of image { RGB (0), GRAY (1), INDEXED (2) }

gimp-image-new-with-precision.blurb: Creates a new image with the specified width, height, type and precision.
gimp-image-new-with-precision.help: Creates a new image, undisplayed with the specified extents, type and precision. Indexed images can only be created at GIMP_PRECISION_U8_GAMMA precision. See 'gimp-image-new' for further details.

args.name: width
args.desc: The width of the image (1 <= width <= 524288)

args.name: height
args.desc: The height of the image (1 <= height <= 524288)

args.name: type
args.desc: The type of image { RGB (0), GRAY (1), INDEXED (2) }

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision { PRECISION-U8-LINEAR (100), PRECISION-U8-GAMMA (150), PRECISION-U16-LINEAR (200), PRECISION-U16-GAMMA (250), PRECISION-U32-LINEAR (300), PRECISION-U32-GAMMA (350), PRECISION-HALF-LINEAR (500), PRECISION-HALF-GAMMA (550), PRECISION-FLOAT-LINEAR (600), PRECISION-FLOAT-GAMMA (650), PRECISION-DOUBLE-LINEAR (700), PRECISION-DOUBLE-GAMMA (750) }

gimp-image-parasite-attach.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-attach-parasite' instead.
gimp-image-parasite-attach.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-attach-parasite' instead.

args.name: parasite
args.desc: The parasite to attach to an image

gimp-image-parasite-detach.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-detach-parasite' instead.
gimp-image-parasite-detach.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-detach-parasite' instead.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach from an image.

gimp-image-parasite-find.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-parasite' instead.
gimp-image-parasite-find.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-parasite' instead.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-image-parasite-list.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-parasite-list' instead.
gimp-image-parasite-list.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-parasite-list' instead.

gimp-image-pick-color.blurb: Determine the color at the given drawable coordinates
gimp-image-pick-color.help: This tool determines the color at the specified coordinates. The returned color is an RGB triplet even for grayscale and indexed drawables. If the coordinates lie outside of the extents of the specified drawable, then an error is returned. If the drawable has an alpha channel, the algorithm examines the alpha value of the drawable at the coordinates. If the alpha value is completely transparent (0), then an error is returned. If the sample_merged parameter is TRUE, the data of the composite image will be used instead of that for the specified drawable. This is equivalent to sampling for colors after merging all visible layers. In the case of a merged sampling, the supplied drawable is ignored.

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: sample-average
args.desc: Average the color of all the pixels in a specified radius (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: average-radius
args.desc: The radius of pixels to average (average-radius >= 0)

gimp-image-pick-correlate-layer.blurb: Find the layer visible at the specified coordinates.
gimp-image-pick-correlate-layer.help: This procedure finds the layer which is visible at the specified coordinates. Layers which do not qualify are those whose extents do not pass within the specified coordinates, or which are transparent at the specified coordinates. This procedure will return -1 if no layer is found.

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate for the pick

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate for the pick

gimp-image-raise-channel.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.
gimp-image-raise-channel.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.

gimp-image-raise-item.blurb: Raise the specified item in its level in its item tree
gimp-image-raise-item.help: This procedure raises the specified item one step in the item tree. The procedure call will fail if there is no item above it.

gimp-image-raise-item-to-top.blurb: Raise the specified item to the top of its level in its item tree
gimp-image-raise-item-to-top.help: This procedure raises the specified item to top of its level in the item tree. It will not move the item if there is no item above it.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to raise to top

gimp-image-raise-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.
gimp-image-raise-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.

gimp-image-raise-layer-to-top.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.
gimp-image-raise-layer-to-top.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to raise to top

gimp-image-raise-vectors.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.
gimp-image-raise-vectors.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.

gimp-image-raise-vectors-to-top.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.
gimp-image-raise-vectors-to-top.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to raise to top

gimp-image-remove-channel.blurb: Remove the specified channel from the image.
gimp-image-remove-channel.help: This procedure removes the specified channel from the image. If the channel doesn't exist, an error is returned.

gimp-image-remove-layer.blurb: Remove the specified layer from the image.
gimp-image-remove-layer.help: This procedure removes the specified layer from the image. If the layer doesn't exist, an error is returned. If there are no layers left in the image, this call will fail. If this layer is the last layer remaining, the image will become empty and have no active layer.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer from which to remove mask

gimp-image-remove-vectors.blurb: Remove the specified path from the image.
gimp-image-remove-vectors.help: This procedure removes the specified path from the image. If the path doesn't exist, an error is returned.

gimp-image-reorder-item.blurb: Reorder the specified item within its item tree
gimp-image-reorder-item.help: This procedure reorders the specified item within its item tree.

args.name: position
args.desc: The new position of the item

gimp-image-resize.blurb: Resize the image to the specified extents.
gimp-image-resize.help: This procedure resizes the image so that it's new width and height are equal to the supplied parameters. Offsets are also provided which describe the position of the previous image's content. All channels within the image are resized according to the specified parameters; this includes the image selection mask. All layers within the image are repositioned according to the specified offsets.

args.name: offx
args.desc: x offset between upper left corner of old and new images: (new - old)

args.name: offy
args.desc: y offset between upper left corner of old and new images: (new - old)

gimp-image-resize-to-layers.blurb: Resize the image to fit all layers.
gimp-image-resize-to-layers.help: This procedure resizes the image to the bounding box of all layers of the image. All channels within the image are resized to the new size; this includes the image selection mask. All layers within the image are repositioned to the new image area.

gimp-image-rotate.blurb: Rotates the image by the specified degrees.

args.name: rotate-type
args.desc: Angle of rotation { ROTATE-90 (0), ROTATE-180 (1), ROTATE-270 (2) }

gimp-image-scale.blurb: Scale the image using the default interpolation method.
gimp-image-scale.help: This procedure scales the image so that its new width and height are equal to the supplied parameters. All layers and channels within the image are scaled according to the specified parameters; this includes the image selection mask. The interpolation method used can be set with 'gimp-context-set-interpolation'.

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Type of interpolation { INTERPOLATION-NONE (0), INTERPOLATION-LINEAR (1), INTERPOLATION-CUBIC (2), INTERPOLATION-NOHALO (3), INTERPOLATION-LOHALO (4) }

gimp-image-select-color.blurb: Create a selection by selecting all pixels (in the specified drawable) with the same (or similar) color to that specified.
gimp-image-select-color.help:
This tool creates a selection over the specified image. A by-color selection is determined by the supplied color under the constraints of the current context settings. Essentially, all pixels (in the drawable) that have color sufficiently close to the specified color (as determined by the threshold and criterion context values) are included in the selection. To select transparent regions, the color specified must also have minimum alpha.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-transparent'.

In the case of a merged sampling, the supplied drawable is ignored.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-image-select-contiguous-color.blurb: Create a selection by selecting all pixels around specified coordinates with the same (or similar) color to that at the coordinates.
gimp-image-select-contiguous-color.help:
This tool creates a contiguous selection over the specified image. A contiguous color selection is determined by a seed fill under the constraints of the current context settings. Essentially, the color at the specified coordinates (in the drawable) is measured and the selection expands outwards from that point to any adjacent pixels which are not significantly different (as determined by the threshold and criterion context settings). This process continues until no more expansion is possible. If antialiasing is turned on, the final selection mask will contain intermediate values based on close misses to the threshold bar at pixels along the seed fill boundary.
