This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-transparent', 'gimp-context-set-diagonal-neighbors'.

In the case of a merged sampling, the supplied drawable is ignored. If the sample is merged, the specified coordinates are relative to the image origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of initial seed fill point: (image coordinates)

gimp-image-select-ellipse.blurb: Create an elliptical selection over the specified image.
gimp-image-select-ellipse.help:
This tool creates an elliptical selection over the specified image. The elliptical region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of ellipse bounding box

gimp-image-select-item.blurb: Transforms the specified item into a selection
gimp-image-select-item.help:
This procedure renders the item's outline into the current selection of the image the item belongs to. What exactly the item's outline is depends on the item type: for layers, it's the layer's alpha channel, for vectors the vector's shape.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to render to the selection

gimp-image-select-polygon.blurb: Create a polygonal selection over the specified image.
gimp-image-select-polygon.help:
This tool creates a polygonal selection over the specified image. The polygonal region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask. The polygon is specified through an array of floating point numbers and its length. The length of array must be 2n, where n is the number of points. Each point is defined by 2 floating point values which correspond to the x and y coordinates. If the final point does not connect to the starting point, a connecting segment is automatically added.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: num-segs
args.desc: Number of points (count 1 coordinate as two points) (num-segs >= 2)

args.name: segs
args.desc: Array of points: { p1.x, p1.y, p2.x, p2.y, ..., pn.x, pn.y}

gimp-image-select-rectangle.blurb: Create a rectangular selection over the specified image;
gimp-image-select-rectangle.help:
This tool creates a rectangular selection over the specified image. The rectangular region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

gimp-image-select-round-rectangle.blurb: Create a rectangular selection with round corners over the specified image;
gimp-image-select-round-rectangle.help:
This tool creates a rectangular selection with round corners over the specified image. The rectangular region can be either added to, subtracted from, or replace the contents of the previous selection mask.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-antialias', 'gimp-context-set-feather', 'gimp-context-set-feather-radius'.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: corner-radius-x
args.desc: The corner radius in X direction (0 <= corner-radius-x <= 524288)

args.name: corner-radius-y
args.desc: The corner radius in Y direction (0 <= corner-radius-y <= 524288)

gimp-image-set-active-channel.blurb: Sets the specified image's active channel.
gimp-image-set-active-channel.help: If the channel exists, it is set as the active channel in the image. Any previous active channel or layer is set to inactive. An exception is a previously existing floating selection, in which case this procedure will return an execution error.

gimp-image-set-active-layer.blurb: Sets the specified image's active layer.
gimp-image-set-active-layer.help: If the layer exists, it is set as the active layer in the image. Any previous active layer or channel is set to inactive. An exception is a previously existing floating selection, in which case this procedure will return an execution error.

gimp-image-set-active-vectors.blurb: Sets the specified image's active vectors.
gimp-image-set-active-vectors.help: If the path exists, it is set as the active path in the image.

gimp-image-set-cmap.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-set-colormap' instead.
gimp-image-set-cmap.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-set-colormap' instead.

args.name: num-bytes
args.desc: Number of bytes in the colormap array (0 <= num-bytes <= 768)

gimp-image-set-color-profile.help: This procedure sets the image's color profile, or unsets it if NULL is passed as 'color_profile'. This procedure does no color conversion.

args.name: num-bytes
args.desc: Number of bytes in the color_profile array (num-bytes >= 0)

gimp-image-set-color-profile-from-file.blurb: Sets the image's color profile from an ICC file
gimp-image-set-color-profile-from-file.help: This procedure sets the image's color profile from a file containing an ICC profile, or unsets it if NULL is passed as 'uri'. This procedure does no color conversion.

args.name: uri
args.desc: The URI of the file containing the new color profile

gimp-image-set-colormap.blurb: Sets the entries in the image's colormap.
gimp-image-set-colormap.help: This procedure sets the entries in the specified image's colormap. The number of entries is specified by the 'num-bytes' parameter and corresponds to the number of INT8 triples that must be contained in the 'colormap' array. The actual number of colors in the transmitted colormap is 'num-bytes' / 3.

args.name: num-bytes
args.desc: Number of bytes in the colormap array (0 <= num-bytes <= 768)

gimp-image-set-component-active.blurb: Sets if the specified image's image component is active.
gimp-image-set-component-active.help: This procedure sets if the specified image's image component (i.e. Red, Green, Blue intensity channels in an RGB image) is active or inactive -- whether or not it can be modified. If the specified component is not valid for the image type, an error is returned.

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }

gimp-image-set-component-visible.blurb: Sets if the specified image's image component is visible.
gimp-image-set-component-visible.help: This procedure sets if the specified image's image component (i.e. Red, Green, Blue intensity channels in an RGB image) is visible or invisible -- whether or not it can be seen. If the specified component is not valid for the image type, an error is returned.

args.name: component
args.desc: The image component { CHANNEL-RED (0), CHANNEL-GREEN (1), CHANNEL-BLUE (2), CHANNEL-GRAY (3), CHANNEL-INDEXED (4), CHANNEL-ALPHA (5) }

gimp-image-set-filename.help: This procedure sets the specified image's filename. The filename should be in the filesystem encoding.

gimp-image-set-metadata.help: Sets exif/iptc/xmp metadata on the image.

args.name: metadata-string
args.desc: The exif/ptc/xmp metadata as a string

gimp-image-set-resolution.help: This procedure sets the specified image's resolution in dots per inch. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image. No scaling or resizing is performed.

args.name: xresolution
args.desc: The new image resolution in the x-axis, in dots per inch

args.name: yresolution
args.desc: The new image resolution in the y-axis, in dots per inch

gimp-image-set-tattoo-state.blurb: Set the tattoo state associated with the image.
gimp-image-set-tattoo-state.help: This procedure sets the tattoo state of the image. Use only by save/load plug-ins that wish to preserve an images tattoo state. Using this function at other times will produce unexpected results. A full check of uniqueness of states in layers, channels and paths will be performed by this procedure and a execution failure will be returned if this fails. A failure will also be returned if the new tattoo state value is less than the maximum tattoo value from all of the tattoos from the paths, layers and channels. After the image data has been loaded and all the tattoos have been set then this is the last procedure that should be called. If effectively does a status check on the tattoo values that have been set to make sure that all is OK.

gimp-image-set-unit.help: This procedure sets the specified image's unit. No scaling or resizing is performed. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image. See the gimp_unit_*() procedure definitions for the valid range of unit IDs and a description of the unit system.

gimp-image-thaw-channels.help:
This procedure thaws the channel list of the image, re-enabling updates to the Channels dialog.

This procedure should match a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-freeze-channels'.

gimp-image-thaw-layers.help:
This procedure thaws the layer list of the image, re-enabling updates to the Layers dialog.

This procedure should match a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-freeze-layers'.

gimp-image-thaw-vectors.help:
This procedure thaws the vectors list of the image, re-enabling updates to the Paths dialog.

This procedure should match a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-freeze-vectors'.

gimp-image-thumbnail.blurb: Get a thumbnail of an image.
gimp-image-thumbnail.help: This function gets data from which a thumbnail of an image preview can be created. Maximum x or y dimension is 1024 pixels. The pixels are returned in RGB[A] or GRAY[A] format. The bpp return value gives the number of bits per pixel in the image.

gimp-image-undo-disable.help: This procedure disables the image's undo stack, allowing subsequent operations to ignore their undo steps. This is generally called in conjunction with 'gimp-image-undo-enable' to temporarily disable an image undo stack. This is advantageous because saving undo steps can be time and memory intensive.

gimp-image-undo-enable.help: This procedure enables the image's undo stack, allowing subsequent operations to store their undo steps. This is generally called in conjunction with 'gimp-image-undo-disable' to temporarily disable an image undo stack.

gimp-image-undo-freeze.help: This procedure freezes the image's undo stack, allowing subsequent operations to ignore their undo steps. This is generally called in conjunction with 'gimp-image-undo-thaw' to temporarily disable an image undo stack. This is advantageous because saving undo steps can be time and memory intensive. 'gimp-image-undo-freeze' / 'gimp-image-undo-thaw' and 'gimp-image-undo-disable' / 'gimp-image-undo-enable' differ in that the former does not free up all undo steps when undo is thawed, so is more suited to interactive in-situ previews. It is important in this case that the image is back to the same state it was frozen in before thawing, else 'undo' behaviour is undefined.

gimp-image-undo-group-end.help: This function must be called once for each 'gimp-image-undo-group-start' call that is made.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to close an undo group

gimp-image-undo-group-start.help: This function is used to start a group undo--necessary for logically combining two or more undo operations into a single operation. This call must be used in conjunction with a 'gimp-image-undo-group-end' call.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to open an undo group

gimp-image-undo-is-enabled.blurb: Check if the image's undo stack is enabled.
gimp-image-undo-is-enabled.help: This procedure checks if the image's undo stack is currently enabled or disabled. This is useful when several plug-ins or scripts call each other and want to check if their caller has already used 'gimp-image-undo-disable' or 'gimp-image-undo-freeze'.

gimp-image-undo-thaw.help: This procedure thaws the image's undo stack, allowing subsequent operations to store their undo steps. This is generally called in conjunction with 'gimp-image-undo-freeze' to temporarily freeze an image undo stack. 'gimp-image-undo-thaw' does NOT free the undo stack as 'gimp-image-undo-enable' does, so is suited for situations where one wishes to leave the undo stack in the same state in which one found it despite non-destructively playing with the image in the meantime. An example would be in-situ plug-in previews. Balancing freezes and thaws and ensuring image consistency is the responsibility of the caller.

gimp-image-unset-active-channel.blurb: Unsets the active channel in the specified image.
gimp-image-unset-active-channel.help: If an active channel exists, it is unset. There then exists no active channel, and if desired, one can be set through a call to 'Set Active Channel'. No error is returned in the case of no existing active channel.

gimp-image-width.blurb: Return the width of the image
gimp-image-width.help: This procedure returns the image's width. This value is independent of any of the layers in this image. This is the "canvas" width.

gimp-item-attach-parasite.blurb: Add a parasite to an item.
gimp-item-attach-parasite.help: This procedure attaches a parasite to an item. It has no return values.

args.name: parasite
args.desc: The parasite to attach to the item

gimp-item-delete.help: This procedure deletes the specified item. This must not be done if the image containing this item was already deleted or if the item was already removed from the image. The only case in which this procedure is useful is if you want to get rid of a item which has not yet been added to an image.

gimp-item-detach-parasite.blurb: Removes a parasite from an item.
gimp-item-detach-parasite.help: This procedure detaches a parasite from an item. It has no return values.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach from the item.

gimp-item-get-children.blurb: Returns the item's list of children.
gimp-item-get-children.help: This procedure returns the list of items which are children of the specified item. The order is topmost to bottommost.

gimp-item-get-color-tag.blurb: Get the color tag of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-color-tag.help: This procedure returns the specified item's color tag.

gimp-item-get-expanded.blurb: Returns whether the item is expanded.
gimp-item-get-expanded.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is expanded.

gimp-item-get-image.help: This procedure returns the item's image.

gimp-item-get-linked.blurb: Get the linked state of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-linked.help: This procedure returns the specified item's linked state.

gimp-item-get-lock-content.blurb: Get the 'lock content' state of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-lock-content.help: This procedure returns the specified item's lock content state.

gimp-item-get-lock-position.blurb: Get the 'lock position' state of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-lock-position.help: This procedure returns the specified item's lock position state.

gimp-item-get-name.blurb: Get the name of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-name.help: This procedure returns the specified item's name.

gimp-item-get-parasite.blurb: Look up a parasite in an item
gimp-item-get-parasite.help: Finds and returns the parasite that is attached to an item.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-item-get-parasite-list.help: Returns a list of all parasites currently attached the an item.

gimp-item-get-parent.help: This procedure returns the item's parent item, if any.

gimp-item-get-tattoo.blurb: Get the tattoo of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-tattoo.help: This procedure returns the specified item's tattoo. A tattoo is a unique and permanent identifier attached to a item that can be used to uniquely identify a item within an image even between sessions.

gimp-item-get-visible.blurb: Get the visibility of the specified item.
gimp-item-get-visible.help: This procedure returns the specified item's visibility.

gimp-item-is-channel.blurb: Returns whether the item is a channel.
gimp-item-is-channel.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a channel.

gimp-item-is-drawable.blurb: Returns whether the item is a drawable.
gimp-item-is-drawable.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a drawable.

gimp-item-is-group.blurb: Returns whether the item is a group item.
gimp-item-is-group.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a group item which can have children.

gimp-item-is-layer.blurb: Returns whether the item is a layer.
gimp-item-is-layer.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a layer.

gimp-item-is-layer-mask.blurb: Returns whether the item is a layer mask.
gimp-item-is-layer-mask.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a layer mask.

gimp-item-is-selection.blurb: Returns whether the item is a selection.
gimp-item-is-selection.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a selection.

gimp-item-is-text-layer.blurb: Returns whether the item is a text layer.
gimp-item-is-text-layer.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a text layer.

gimp-item-is-valid.blurb: Returns TRUE if the item is valid.
gimp-item-is-valid.help: This procedure checks if the given item ID is valid and refers to an existing item.

gimp-item-is-vectors.blurb: Returns whether the item is a vectors.
gimp-item-is-vectors.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the specified item is a vectors.

gimp-item-set-color-tag.blurb: Set the color tag of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-color-tag.help: This procedure sets the specified item's color tag.

args.name: color-tag
args.desc: The new item color tag { COLOR-TAG-NONE (0), COLOR-TAG-BLUE (1), COLOR-TAG-GREEN (2), COLOR-TAG-YELLOW (3), COLOR-TAG-ORANGE (4), COLOR-TAG-BROWN (5), COLOR-TAG-RED (6), COLOR-TAG-VIOLET (7), COLOR-TAG-GRAY (8) }

gimp-item-set-expanded.blurb: Sets the expanded state of the item.
gimp-item-set-expanded.help: This procedure expands or collapses the item.

args.name: expanded
args.desc: TRUE to expand the item, FALSE to collapse the item (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-set-linked.blurb: Set the linked state of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-linked.help: This procedure sets the specified item's linked state.

args.name: linked
args.desc: The new item linked state (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-set-lock-content.blurb: Set the 'lock content' state of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-lock-content.help: This procedure sets the specified item's lock content state.

args.name: lock-content
args.desc: The new item 'lock content' state (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-set-lock-position.blurb: Set the 'lock position' state of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-lock-position.help: This procedure sets the specified item's lock position state.

args.name: lock-position
args.desc: The new item 'lock position' state (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-set-name.blurb: Set the name of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-name.help: This procedure sets the specified item's name.

gimp-item-set-tattoo.blurb: Set the tattoo of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-tattoo.help: This procedure sets the specified item's tattoo. A tattoo is a unique and permanent identifier attached to a item that can be used to uniquely identify a item within an image even between sessions.

gimp-item-set-visible.blurb: Set the visibility of the specified item.
gimp-item-set-visible.help: This procedure sets the specified item's visibility.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-transform-2d.help:
This procedure transforms the specified item.

The transformation is done by scaling by the x and y scale factors about the point (source_x, source_y), then rotating around the same point, then translating that point to the new position (dest_x, dest_y).

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then transformed as specified. The return value is the ID of the transformed floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be transformed according to the specified parameters. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be transformed the same way. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: source-x
args.desc: X coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: source-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: scale-x
args.desc: Amount to scale in x direction

args.name: scale-y
args.desc: Amount to scale in y direction

args.name: dest-x
args.desc: X coordinate of where the center goes

args.name: dest-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of where the center goes

gimp-item-transform-flip.blurb: Flip the specified item around a given line.
gimp-item-transform-flip.help:
This procedure flips the specified item.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then flipped. The axis to flip around is specified by specifying two points from that line. The return value is the ID of the flipped floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be flipped around the specified axis. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be flipped around the same axis. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: x0
args.desc: horz. coord. of one end of axis

args.name: y0
args.desc: vert. coord. of one end of axis

args.name: x1
args.desc: horz. coord. of other end of axis

args.name: y1
args.desc: vert. coord. of other end of axis

gimp-item-transform-flip-simple.blurb: Flip the specified item either vertically or horizontally.
gimp-item-transform-flip-simple.help:
This procedure flips the specified item.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then flipped. If auto_center is set to TRUE, the flip is around the selection's center. Otherwise, the coordinate of the axis needs to be specified. The return value is the ID of the flipped floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be flipped around its center if auto_center is set to TRUE, otherwise the coordinate of the axis needs to be specified. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be flipped around the same axis. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: auto-center
args.desc: Whether to automatically position the axis in the selection center (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-item-transform-matrix.help:
This procedure transforms the specified item.

The transformation is done by assembling a 3x3 matrix from the coefficients passed.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then transformed as specified. The return value is the ID of the transformed floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be transformed according to the specified matrix. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be transformed the same way. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

gimp-item-transform-perspective.blurb: Perform a possibly non-affine transformation on the specified item.
gimp-item-transform-perspective.help:
This procedure performs a possibly non-affine transformation on the specified item by allowing the corners of the original bounding box to be arbitrarily remapped to any values.

The 4 coordinates specify the new locations of each corner of the original bounding box. By specifying these values, any affine transformation (rotation, scaling, translation) can be affected. Additionally, these values can be specified such that the resulting transformed item will appear to have been projected via a perspective transform.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then transformed as specified. The return value is the ID of the transformed floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be transformed according to the specified mapping. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be transformed the same way. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: x2
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y2
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x3
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y3
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

gimp-item-transform-rotate.blurb: Rotate the specified item about given coordinates through the specified angle.
gimp-item-transform-rotate.help:
This function rotates the specified item.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then rotated by the specified amount. If auto_center is set to TRUE, the rotation is around the selection's center. Otherwise, the coordinate of the center point needs to be specified. The return value is the ID of the rotated floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be rotated around its center if auto_center is set to TRUE, otherwise the coordinate of the center point needs to be specified. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be rotated around the same center point. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: auto-center
args.desc: Whether to automatically rotate around the selection center (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: center-x
args.desc: The hor. coordinate of the center of rotation

args.name: center-y
args.desc: The vert. coordinate of the center of rotation

gimp-item-transform-rotate-simple.blurb: Rotate the specified item about given coordinates through the specified angle.
gimp-item-transform-rotate-simple.help:
This function rotates the specified item.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then rotated by the specified amount. If auto_center is set to TRUE, the rotation is around the selection's center. Otherwise, the coordinate of the center point needs to be specified. The return value is the ID of the rotated floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be rotated around its center if auto_center is set to TRUE, otherwise the coordinate of the center point needs to be specified. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be rotated around the same center point. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: rotate-type
args.desc: Type of rotation { ROTATE-90 (0), ROTATE-180 (1), ROTATE-270 (2) }

args.name: auto-center
args.desc: Whether to automatically rotate around the selection center (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: center-x
args.desc: The hor. coordinate of the center of rotation

args.name: center-y
args.desc: The vert. coordinate of the center of rotation

gimp-item-transform-scale.help:
This procedure scales the specified item.

The 2 coordinates specify the new locations of the top-left and bottom-roght corners of the original bounding box.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then scaled as specified. The return value is the ID of the scaled floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be scaled according to the specified coordinates. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be scaled the same way. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

gimp-item-transform-shear.blurb: Shear the specified item about its center by the specified magnitude.
gimp-item-transform-shear.help:
This procedure shears the specified item.

The shear type parameter indicates whether the shear will be applied horizontally or vertically. The magnitude can be either positive or negative and indicates the extent (in pixels) to shear by.

If a selection exists and the item is a drawable, the portion of the drawable which lies under the selection is cut from the drawable and made into a floating selection which is then sheared as specified. The return value is the ID of the sheared floating selection.

If there is no selection or the item is not a drawable, the entire item will be sheared according to the specified parameters. Additionally, if the item has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be sheared the same way. The return value will be equal to the item ID supplied as input.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-interpolation', 'gimp-context-set-transform-direction', 'gimp-context-set-transform-resize'.

gimp-item-transform-translate.blurb: Translate the item by the specified offsets.
gimp-item-transform-translate.help:
This procedure translates the item by the amounts specified in the off_x and off_y arguments. These can be negative, and are considered offsets from the current position. The offsets will be rounded to the nearest pixel unless the item is a path.

If the item is attached to an image and has its linked flag set to TRUE, all additional items contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE will also be translated by the specified offsets.

gimp-layer-add-alpha.blurb: Add an alpha channel to the layer if it doesn't already have one.
gimp-layer-add-alpha.help: This procedure adds an additional component to the specified layer if it does not already possess an alpha channel. An alpha channel makes it possible to clear and erase to transparency, instead of the background color. This transforms layers of type RGB to RGBA, GRAY to GRAYA, and INDEXED to INDEXEDA.

gimp-layer-add-mask.blurb: Add a layer mask to the specified layer.
gimp-layer-add-mask.help: This procedure adds a layer mask to the specified layer. Layer masks serve as an additional alpha channel for a layer. This procedure will fail if a number of prerequisites aren't met. The layer cannot already have a layer mask. The specified mask must exist and have the same dimensions as the layer. The layer must have been created for use with the specified image and the mask must have been created with the procedure 'gimp-layer-create-mask'.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to receive the mask

args.name: mask
args.desc: The mask to add to the layer

gimp-layer-copy.help: This procedure copies the specified layer and returns the copy. The newly copied layer is for use within the original layer's image. It should not be subsequently added to any other image. The copied layer can optionally have an added alpha channel. This is useful if the background layer in an image is being copied and added to the same image.

args.name: add-alpha
args.desc: Add an alpha channel to the copied layer (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-create-mask.blurb: Create a layer mask for the specified layer.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to which to add the mask

args.name: mask-type
args.desc: The type of mask { ADD-MASK-WHITE (0), ADD-MASK-BLACK (1), ADD-MASK-ALPHA (2), ADD-MASK-ALPHA-TRANSFER (3), ADD-MASK-SELECTION (4), ADD-MASK-COPY (5), ADD-MASK-CHANNEL (6) }

gimp-layer-delete.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.
gimp-layer-delete.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.

gimp-layer-flatten.blurb: Remove the alpha channel from the layer if it has one.
gimp-layer-flatten.help: This procedure removes the alpha channel from a layer, blending all (partially) transparent pixels in the layer against the background color. This transforms layers of type RGBA to RGB, GRAYA to GRAY, and INDEXEDA to INDEXED.

gimp-layer-from-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified mask's layer , or -1 if none exists.

args.name: mask
args.desc: Mask for which to return the layer

gimp-layer-get-apply-mask.blurb: Get the apply mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-apply-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's apply mask setting. If the value is TRUE, then the layer mask for this layer is currently being composited with the layer's alpha channel.

gimp-layer-get-blend-space.blurb: Get the blend space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-blend-space.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's blend space.

gimp-layer-get-composite-mode.blurb: Get the composite mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-composite-mode.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's composite mode.

gimp-layer-get-composite-space.blurb: Get the composite space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-composite-space.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's composite space.

gimp-layer-get-edit-mask.blurb: Get the edit mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-edit-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's edit mask setting. If the value is TRUE, then the layer mask for this layer is currently active, and not the layer.

gimp-layer-get-linked.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.
gimp-layer-get-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.

gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha.blurb: Get the lock alpha channel setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's lock alpha channel setting.

gimp-layer-get-mask.blurb: Get the specified layer's mask if it exists.
gimp-layer-get-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's mask, or -1 if none exists.

gimp-layer-get-mode.blurb: Get the combination mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-mode.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's combination mode.

gimp-layer-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-layer-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-layer-get-opacity.blurb: Get the opacity of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-opacity.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's opacity.

gimp-layer-get-preserve-trans.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha' instead.
gimp-layer-get-preserve-trans.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha' instead.

gimp-layer-get-show-mask.blurb: Get the show mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-show-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's show mask setting. This controls whether the layer or its mask is visible. TRUE indicates that the mask should be visible. If the layer has no mask, then this function returns an error.

gimp-layer-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-layer-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-layer-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-layer-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-layer-group-new.help:
This procedure creates a new layer group. Attributes such as layer mode and opacity should be set with explicit procedure calls. Add the new layer group (which is a kind of layer) with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command.
Other procedures useful with layer groups: 'gimp-image-reorder-item', 'gimp-item-get-parent', 'gimp-item-get-children', 'gimp-item-is-group'.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the layer group

gimp-layer-is-floating-sel.blurb: Is the specified layer a floating selection?
gimp-layer-is-floating-sel.help: This procedure returns whether the layer is a floating selection. Floating selections are special cases of layers which are attached to a specific drawable.

gimp-layer-mask.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-mask' instead.
gimp-layer-mask.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-mask' instead.

gimp-layer-new.help: This procedure creates a new layer with the specified width, height, and type. Name, opacity, and mode are also supplied parameters. The new layer still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the layer

args.name: type
args.desc: The layer type { RGB-IMAGE (0), RGBA-IMAGE (1), GRAY-IMAGE (2), GRAYA-IMAGE (3), INDEXED-IMAGE (4), INDEXEDA-IMAGE (5) }

args.name: mode
args.desc: The layer combination mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

gimp-layer-new-from-drawable.blurb: Create a new layer by copying an existing drawable.
gimp-layer-new-from-drawable.help: This procedure creates a new layer as a copy of the specified drawable. The new layer still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The source drawable from where the new layer is copied

args.name: dest-image
args.desc: The destination image to which to add the layer

gimp-layer-new-from-visible.blurb: Create a new layer from what is visible in an image.
gimp-layer-new-from-visible.help: This procedure creates a new layer from what is visible in the given image. The new layer still needs to be added to the destination image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The source image from where the content is copied

args.name: dest-image

The 'local-origin' parameter specifies whether to scale from the center of the layer, or from the image origin. This operation only works if the layer has been added to an image. The interpolation method used can be set with 'gimp-context-set-interpolation'.

gimp-paintbrush.blurb: Paint in the current brush with optional fade out parameter and pull colors from a gradient.
gimp-paintbrush.help: This tool is the standard paintbrush. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The 'fade-out' parameter is measured in pixels and allows the brush stroke to linearly fall off. The pressure is set to the maximum at the beginning of the stroke. As the distance of the stroke nears the fade-out value, the pressure will approach zero. The gradient-length is the distance to spread the gradient over. It is measured in pixels. If the gradient-length is 0, no gradient is used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: method
args.desc: The paint method to use { PAINT-CONSTANT (0), PAINT-INCREMENTAL (1) }

gimp-paintbrush-default.blurb: Paint in the current brush. The fade out parameter and pull colors from a gradient parameter are set from the paintbrush options dialog. If this dialog has not been activated then the dialog defaults will be used.
gimp-paintbrush-default.help: This tool is similar to the standard paintbrush. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The 'fade-out' parameter is measured in pixels and allows the brush stroke to linearly fall off (value obtained from the option dialog). The pressure is set to the maximum at the beginning of the stroke. As the distance of the stroke nears the fade-out value, the pressure will approach zero. The gradient-length (value obtained from the option dialog) is the distance to spread the gradient over. It is measured in pixels. If the gradient-length is 0, no gradient is used.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: image

args.desc: The name of the path whose tattoo should be obtained.

args.desc: The name of the SVG file to import.

args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.desc: The image to list the paths from

args.desc: The image in which a path will become current

args.name: name
args.desc: the name of the path whose locked status should be set

args.name: locked
args.desc: Whether the path is locked (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to set the paths in

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path to create. If it exists then a unique name will be created - query the list of paths if you want to make sure that the name of the path you create is unique. This will be set as the current path.

args.name: ptype
args.desc: The type of the path. Currently only one type (1 = Bezier) is supported.

args.name: num-path-points
args.desc: The number of elements in the array, i.e. the number of points in the path * 3. Each point is made up of (x, y, type) of floats. Currently only the creation of bezier curves is allowed. The type parameter must be set to (1) to indicate a BEZIER type curve. Note that for BEZIER curves, points must be given in the following order: ACCACCAC... If the path is not closed the last control point is missed off. Points consist of three control points (control/anchor/control) so for a curve that is not closed there must be at least two points passed (2 x,y pairs). If (num_path_points/3) % 3 = 0 then the path is assumed to be closed and the points are ACCACCACCACC. (num-path-points >= 0)

args.name: points-pairs
args.desc: The points in the path represented as 3 floats. The first is the x pos, next is the y pos, last is the type of the pnt. The type field is dependent on the path type. For beziers (type 1 paths) the type can either be (1.0 = BEZIER_ANCHOR, 2.0 = BEZIER_CONTROL, 3.0= BEZIER_MOVE). Note all points are returned in pixel resolution.

args.name: name
args.desc: the name of the path whose tattoo should be set

args.name: tattovalue
args.desc: The tattoo associated with the name path. Only values returned from 'path_get_tattoo' should be used here

args.name: image
args.desc: The image which contains the path to stroke

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the path which should be made into selection.

args.name: op
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }
