args.name: pattern-name
args.desc: The name of the pattern to set as selected

gimp-pencil.blurb: Paint in the current brush without sub-pixel sampling.
gimp-pencil.help: This tool is the standard pencil. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The brush mask is treated as though it contains only black and white values. Any value below half is treated as black; any above half, as white.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: x2
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y2
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x3
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y3
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

gimp-plugin-domain-register.help: This procedure adds a textdomain to the list of domains Gimp searches for strings when translating its menu entries. There is no need to call this function for plug-ins that have their strings included in the 'gimp-std-plugins' domain as that is used by default. If the compiled message catalog is not in the standard location, you may specify an absolute path to another location. This procedure can only be called in the query function of a plug-in and it has to be called before any procedure is installed.

args.name: domain-name
args.desc: The name of the textdomain (must be unique)

args.name: domain-path
args.desc: The absolute path to the compiled message catalog (may be NULL)

gimp-plugin-enable-precision.blurb: Switches this plug-in to using the real bit depth of drawables.
gimp-plugin-enable-precision.help: Switches this plug-in to using the real bit depth of drawables. This setting can only be enabled, and not disabled again during the lifetime of the plug-in. Using 'gimp-drawable-get-buffer', 'gimp-drawable-get-shadow-buffer' or 'gimp-drawable-get-format' will automatically call this function.

gimp-plugin-get-pdb-error-handler.blurb: Retrieves the active error handler for procedure calls.
gimp-plugin-get-pdb-error-handler.help: This procedure retrieves the currently active error handler for procedure calls made by the calling plug-in. See 'gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler' for details.

gimp-plugin-help-register.blurb: Register a help path for a plug-in.
gimp-plugin-help-register.help: This procedure registers user documentation for the calling plug-in with the GIMP help system. The domain_uri parameter points to the root directory where the plug-in help is installed. For each supported language there should be a file called 'gimp-help.xml' that maps the help IDs to the actual help files.

args.name: domain-name
args.desc: The XML namespace of the plug-in's help pages

args.name: domain-uri
args.desc: The root URI of the plug-in's help pages

gimp-plugin-icon-register.blurb: Register an icon for a plug-in procedure.
gimp-plugin-icon-register.help: This procedure installs an icon for the given procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure for which to install the icon

args.name: icon-type
args.desc: The type of the icon { ICON-TYPE-ICON-NAME (0), ICON-TYPE-INLINE-PIXBUF (1), ICON-TYPE-IMAGE-FILE (2) }

args.name: icon-data
args.desc: The procedure's icon. The format depends on the 'icon_type' parameter

gimp-plugin-menu-branch-register.help: This procedure installs a sub-menu which does not belong to any procedure. The menu-name should be the untranslated menu label. GIMP will look up the translation in the textdomain registered for the plug-in.

gimp-plugin-menu-register.blurb: Register an additional menu path for a plug-in procedure.
gimp-plugin-menu-register.help: This procedure installs an additional menu entry for the given procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure for which to install the menu path

gimp-plugin-precision-enabled.blurb: Whether this plug-in is using the real bit depth of drawables.
gimp-plugin-precision-enabled.help: Returns whether this plug-in is using the real bit depth of drawables, which can be more than 8 bits per channel.

gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler.blurb: Sets an error handler for procedure calls.
gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler.help: This procedure changes the way that errors in procedure calls are handled. By default GIMP will raise an error dialog if a procedure call made by a plug-in fails. Using this procedure the plug-in can change this behavior. If the error handler is set to %GIMP_PDB_ERROR_HANDLER_PLUGIN, then the plug-in is responsible for calling 'gimp-get-pdb-error' and handling the error whenever one if its procedure calls fails. It can do this by displaying the error message or by forwarding it in its own return values.

args.name: handler
args.desc: Who is responsible for handling procedure call errors { PDB-ERROR-HANDLER-INTERNAL (0), PDB-ERROR-HANDLER-PLUGIN (1) }

gimp-plugins-query.blurb: Queries the plug-in database for its contents.
gimp-plugins-query.help: This procedure queries the contents of the plug-in database.

args.name: search-string
args.desc: If not an empty string then use this as a search pattern

gimp-procedural-db-dump.blurb: Dumps the current contents of the procedural database
gimp-procedural-db-dump.help: This procedure dumps the contents of the procedural database to the specified file. The file will contain all of the information provided for each registered procedure.

gimp-procedural-db-get-data.blurb: Returns data associated with the specified identifier.
gimp-procedural-db-get-data.help: This procedure returns any data which may have been associated with the specified identifier. The data is a variable length array of bytes. If no data has been associated with the identifier, an error is returned.

gimp-procedural-db-get-data-size.blurb: Returns size of data associated with the specified identifier.
gimp-procedural-db-get-data-size.help: This procedure returns the size of any data which may have been associated with the specified identifier. If no data has been associated with the identifier, an error is returned.

gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure's argument.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure's argument. The argument type, name, and a description are retrieved.

gimp-procedural-db-proc-exists.blurb: Checks if the specified procedure exists in the procedural database
gimp-procedural-db-proc-exists.help: This procedure checks if the specified procedure is registered in the procedural database.

gimp-procedural-db-proc-info.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-info.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure. A short blurb, detailed help, author(s), copyright information, procedure type, number of input, and number of return values are returned. For specific information on each input argument and return value, use the 'gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg' and 'gimp-procedural-db-proc-val' procedures.

gimp-procedural-db-proc-val.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure's return value.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-val.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure's return value. The return value type, name, and a description are retrieved.

gimp-procedural-db-query.blurb: Queries the procedural database for its contents using regular expression matching.
gimp-procedural-db-query.help: This procedure queries the contents of the procedural database. It is supplied with seven arguments matching procedures on { name, blurb, help, author, copyright, date, procedure type}. This is accomplished using regular expression matching. For instance, to find all procedures with "jpeg" listed in the blurb, all seven arguments can be supplied as ".*", except for the second, which can be supplied as ".*jpeg.*". There are two return arguments for this procedure. The first is the number of procedures matching the query. The second is a concatenated list of procedure names corresponding to those matching the query. If no matching entries are found, then the returned string is NULL and the number of entries is 0.

args.name: proc-type
args.desc: The regex for procedure type: { 'Internal GIMP procedure', 'GIMP Plug-in', 'GIMP Extension', 'Temporary Procedure' }

gimp-procedural-db-set-data.blurb: Associates the specified identifier with the supplied data.
gimp-procedural-db-set-data.help: This procedure associates the supplied data with the provided identifier. The data may be subsequently retrieved by a call to 'procedural-db-get-data'.

args.name: bytes
args.desc: The number of bytes in the data (bytes >= 1)

gimp-procedural-db-temp-name.blurb: Generates a unique temporary PDB name.
gimp-procedural-db-temp-name.help: This procedure generates a temporary PDB entry name that is guaranteed to be unique.

gimp-progress-cancel.help: This function cancels the currently running progress.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this progress

gimp-progress-end.blurb: Ends the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-end.help: Ends the progress display for the current plug-in. Most plug-ins don't need to call this, they just exit when the work is done. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

gimp-progress-get-window-handle.blurb: Returns the native window ID of the toplevel window this plug-in's progress is displayed in.
gimp-progress-get-window-handle.help: This function returns the native window ID of the toplevel window this plug-in's progress is displayed in.

gimp-progress-init.blurb: Initializes the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-init.help: Initializes the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

args.name: message
args.desc: Message to use in the progress dialog

args.name: gdisplay
args.desc: GimpDisplay to update progressbar in, or -1 for a separate window

gimp-progress-install.blurb: Installs a progress callback for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-install.help: This function installs a temporary PDB procedure which will handle all progress calls made by this plug-in and any procedure it calls. Calling this function multiple times simply replaces the old progress callbacks.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The callback PDB proc to call

gimp-progress-pulse.blurb: Pulses the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-pulse.help: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in. Use this function instead of 'gimp-progress-update' if you cannot tell how much progress has been made. This usually causes the the progress bar to enter "activity mode", where a block bounces back and forth.

gimp-progress-set-text.blurb: Changes the text in the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-set-text.help: This function changes the text in the progress bar for the current plug-in. Unlike 'gimp-progress-init' it does not change the displayed value.

args.name: message
args.desc: Message to use in the progress dialog

gimp-progress-uninstall.blurb: Uninstalls the progress callback for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-uninstall.help: This function uninstalls any progress callback installed with 'gimp-progress-install' before.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this progress

gimp-progress-update.blurb: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-update.help: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

args.name: percentage
args.desc: Percentage of progress completed which must be between 0.0 and 1.0

gimp-quit.help: If there are unsaved images in an interactive GIMP session, the user will be asked for confirmation. If force is TRUE, the application is quit without querying the user to save any dirty images.

args.name: force
args.desc: Force GIMP to quit without asking (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-register-file-handler-mime.blurb: Associates MIME types with a file handler procedure.
gimp-register-file-handler-mime.help: Registers MIME types for a file handler procedure. This allows GIMP to determine the MIME type of the file opened or saved using this procedure. It is recommended that only one MIME type is registered per file procedure; when registering more than one MIME type, GIMP will associate the first one with files opened or saved with this procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to associate a MIME type with.

args.name: mime-types
args.desc: A comma-separated list of MIME types, such as "image/jpeg".

gimp-register-file-handler-priority.blurb: Sets the priority of a file handler procedure.
gimp-register-file-handler-priority.help: Sets the priority of a file handler procedure. When more than one procedure matches a given file, the procedure with the lowest priority is used; if more than one procedure has the lowest priority, it is unspecified which one of them is used. The default priority for file handler procedures is 0.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to set the priority of.

gimp-register-file-handler-raw.blurb: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling raw camera files.
gimp-register-file-handler-raw.help: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling raw digital camera files. Use this procedure only to register raw load handlers, calling it on a save handler will generate an error.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to enable raw handling for.

gimp-register-file-handler-uri.blurb: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling URIs.
gimp-register-file-handler-uri.help: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling URIs. This allows GIMP to call the procedure directly for all kinds of URIs, and the 'filename' traditionally passed to file procedures turns into an URI.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to enable URIs for.

gimp-register-load-handler.blurb: Registers a file load handler procedure.
gimp-register-load-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to load files of a particular file format.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for loading

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can load (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can load (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

gimp-register-magic-load-handler.blurb: Registers a file load handler procedure.
gimp-register-magic-load-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to load files of a particular file format using magic file information.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for loading

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can load (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can load (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

args.name: magics
args.desc: comma separated list of magic file information this handler can load (i.e. "0,string,GIF")

gimp-register-save-handler.blurb: Registers a file save handler procedure.
gimp-register-save-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to save files in a particular file format.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for saving

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can save (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can save (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

gimp-register-thumbnail-loader.blurb: Associates a thumbnail loader with a file load procedure.
gimp-register-thumbnail-loader.help: Some file formats allow for embedded thumbnails, other file formats contain a scalable image or provide the image data in different resolutions. A file plug-in for such a format may register a special procedure that allows GIMP to load a thumbnail preview of the image. This procedure is then associated with the standard load procedure using this function.

args.name: load-proc
args.desc: The name of the procedure the thumbnail loader with.

args.name: thumb-proc
args.desc: The name of the thumbnail load procedure.

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: corner-radius-x
args.desc: The corner radius in X direction (0 <= corner-radius-x <= 524288)

args.name: corner-radius-y
args.desc: The corner radius in Y direction (0 <= corner-radius-y <= 524288)

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: feather-radius-x
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in X direction (feather-radius-x >= 0)

args.name: feather-radius-y
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in Y direction (feather-radius-y >= 0)

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

gimp-selection-all.help: This procedure sets the selection mask to completely encompass the image. Every pixel in the selection channel is set to 255.

gimp-selection-border.help: This procedure borders the selection. Bordering creates a new selection which is defined along the boundary of the previous selection at every point within the specified radius.

gimp-selection-bounds.blurb: Find the bounding box of the current selection.
gimp-selection-bounds.help: This procedure returns whether there is a selection for the specified image. If there is one, the upper left and lower right corners of the bounding box are returned. These coordinates are relative to the image. Please note that the pixel specified by the lower right coordinate of the bounding box is not part of the selection. The selection ends at the upper left corner of this pixel. This means the width of the selection can be calculated as (x2 - x1), its height as (y2 - y1).

gimp-selection-clear.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-selection-none' instead.
gimp-selection-clear.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-selection-none' instead.

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-selection-feather.help: This procedure feathers the selection. Feathering is implemented using a gaussian blur.

gimp-selection-float.blurb: Float the selection from the specified drawable with initial offsets as specified.
gimp-selection-float.help: This procedure determines the region of the specified drawable that lies beneath the current selection. The region is then cut from the drawable and the resulting data is made into a new layer which is instantiated as a floating selection. The offsets allow initial positioning of the new floating selection.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable from which to float selection

gimp-selection-flood.blurb: Remove holes from the image's selection
gimp-selection-flood.help: This procedure removes holes from the selection, that can come from selecting a patchy area with the Fuzzy Select Tool. In technical terms this procedure floods the selection. See the Algorithms page in the developer wiki for details.

gimp-selection-grow.help: This procedure grows the selection. Growing involves expanding the boundary in all directions by the specified pixel amount.

gimp-selection-invert.help: This procedure inverts the selection mask. For every pixel in the selection channel, its new value is calculated as (255 - old-value).

gimp-selection-is-empty.blurb: Determine whether the selection is empty.
gimp-selection-is-empty.help: This procedure returns TRUE if the selection for the specified image is empty.

gimp-selection-none.help: This procedure deselects the entire image. Every pixel in the selection channel is set to 0.

gimp-selection-save.blurb: Copy the selection mask to a new channel.
gimp-selection-save.help: This procedure copies the selection mask and stores the content in a new channel. The new channel is automatically inserted into the image's list of channels.

gimp-selection-sharpen.help: This procedure sharpens the selection mask. For every pixel in the selection channel, if the value is > 127, the new pixel is assigned a value of 255. This removes any "anti-aliasing" that might exist in the selection mask's boundary.

gimp-selection-shrink.help: This procedure shrinks the selection. Shrinking involves trimming the existing selection boundary on all sides by the specified number of pixels.

gimp-selection-translate.blurb: Translate the selection by the specified offsets.
gimp-selection-translate.help: This procedure actually translates the selection for the specified image by the specified offsets. Regions that are translated from beyond the bounds of the image are set to empty. Valid regions of the selection which are translated beyond the bounds of the image because of this call are lost.

gimp-selection-value.blurb: Find the value of the selection at the specified coordinates.
gimp-selection-value.help: This procedure returns the value of the selection at the specified coordinates. If the coordinates lie out of bounds, 0 is returned.

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-smudge.help: This tool simulates a smudge using the current brush. High pressure results in a greater smudge of paint while low pressure results in a lesser smudge.

args.name: pressure
args.desc: The pressure of the smudge strokes (0 <= pressure <= 100)

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-smudge-default.help: This tool simulates a smudge using the current brush. It behaves exactly the same as 'gimp-smudge' except that the pressure value is taken from the smudge tool options or the options default if the tools option dialog has not been activated.

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-temp-PDB-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-procedural-db-temp-name' instead.
gimp-temp-PDB-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-procedural-db-temp-name' instead.

gimp-temp-name.help: Generates a unique filename using the temp path supplied in the user's gimprc.

args.name: extension
args.desc: The extension the file will have

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable: (-1 for a new text layer)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate for the left of the text bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate for the top of the text bounding box

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (size >= 0)

args.name: size-type
args.desc: The units of specified size { PIXELS (0), POINTS (1) }

gimp-text-fontname.blurb: Add text at the specified location as a floating selection or a new layer.
gimp-text-fontname.help: This tool requires a fontname matching an installed PangoFT2 font. You can specify the fontsize in units of pixels or points, and the appropriate metric is specified using the size_type argument. The x and y parameters together control the placement of the new text by specifying the upper left corner of the text bounding box. If the specified drawable parameter is valid, the text will be created as a floating selection attached to the drawable. If the drawable parameter is not valid (-1), the text will appear as a new layer. Finally, a border can be specified around the final rendered text. The border is measured in pixels. Parameter size-type is not used and is currently ignored. If you need to display a font in points, divide the size in points by 72.0 and multiply it by the image's vertical resolution.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable: (-1 for a new text layer)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate for the left of the text bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate for the top of the text bounding box

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (size >= 0)

args.name: size-type
args.desc: The units of specified size { PIXELS (0), POINTS (1) }

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (size >= 0)

args.name: size-type
args.desc: The units of specified size { PIXELS (0), POINTS (1) }

gimp-text-get-extents-fontname.blurb: Get extents of the bounding box for the specified text.
gimp-text-get-extents-fontname.help: This tool returns the width and height of a bounding box for the specified text string with the specified font information. Ascent and descent for the specified font are returned as well. Parameter size-type is not used and is currently ignored. If you need to display a font in points, divide the size in points by 72.0 and multiply it by the vertical resolution of the image you are taking into account.

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (size >= 0)

args.name: size-type
args.desc: The units of specified size { PIXELS (0), POINTS (1) }

gimp-text-layer-get-antialias.blurb: Check if antialiasing is used in the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-antialias.help: This procedure checks if antialiasing is enabled in the specified text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-base-direction.blurb: Get the base direction used for rendering the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-base-direction.help: This procedure returns the base direction used for rendering the text in the text layer

gimp-text-layer-get-color.blurb: Get the color of the text in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-color.help: This procedure returns the color of the text in a text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-font.blurb: Get the font from a text layer as string.
gimp-text-layer-get-font.help: This procedure returns the name of the font from a text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-font-size.blurb: Get the font size from a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-font-size.help: This procedure returns the size of the font which is used in a text layer. You will receive the size as a float 'font-size' in 'unit' units.

gimp-text-layer-get-hint-style.blurb: Get information about hinting in the specified text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-hint-style.help: This procedure provides information about the hinting that is being used in a text layer. Hinting can be optimized for fidelity or contrast or it can be turned entirely off.

gimp-text-layer-get-indent.blurb: Get the line indentation of text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-indent.help: This procedure returns the indentation of the first line in a text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-justification.blurb: Get the text justification information of the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-justification.help: This procedure returns the alignment of the lines in the text layer relative to each other.

gimp-text-layer-get-kerning.blurb: Check if kerning is used in the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-kerning.help: This procedure checks if kerning is enabled in the specified text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-language.blurb: Get the language used in the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-language.help: This procedure returns the language string which is set for the text in the text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-letter-spacing.blurb: Get the letter spacing used in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-get-letter-spacing.help: This procedure returns the additional spacing between the single glyphs in a text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-line-spacing.blurb: Get the spacing between lines of text.
gimp-text-layer-get-line-spacing.help: This procedure returns the line-spacing between lines of text in a text layer.

gimp-text-layer-get-markup.blurb: Get the markup from a text layer as string.
gimp-text-layer-get-markup.help: This procedure returns the markup of the styles from a text layer. The markup will be in the form of Pango's markup - See https://www.pango.org/ for more information about Pango and its markup. Note: Setting the markup of a text layer using Pango's markup is not supported for now.

gimp-text-layer-get-text.blurb: Get the text from a text layer as string.
gimp-text-layer-get-text.help: This procedure returns the text from a text layer as a string.

gimp-text-layer-new.help: This procedure creates a new text layer. The arguments are kept as simple as necessary for the normal case. All text attributes, however, can be modified with the appropriate gimp_text_layer_set_*() procedures. The new layer still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer using 'gimp-image-insert-layer'.

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (0 <= size <= 8192)

gimp-text-layer-resize.blurb: Resize the box of a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-resize.help: This procedure changes the width and height of a text layer while keeping it as a text layer and not converting it to a bitmap like 'gimp-layer-resize' would do.

args.name: width
args.desc: The new box width in pixels (0 <= width <= 524288)

args.name: height
args.desc: The new box height in pixels (0 <= height <= 524288)

gimp-text-layer-set-antialias.blurb: Enable/disable anti-aliasing in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-antialias.help: This procedure enables or disables anti-aliasing of the text in a text layer.

args.name: antialias
args.desc: Enable/disable antialiasing of the text (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-text-layer-set-base-direction.blurb: Set the base direction in the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-base-direction.help: This procedure sets the base direction used in applying the Unicode bidirectional algorithm when rendering the text.

args.name: direction
args.desc: The base direction of the text. { TEXT-DIRECTION-LTR (0), TEXT-DIRECTION-RTL (1), TEXT-DIRECTION-TTB-RTL (2), TEXT-DIRECTION-TTB-RTL-UPRIGHT (3), TEXT-DIRECTION-TTB-LTR (4), TEXT-DIRECTION-TTB-LTR-UPRIGHT (5) }

gimp-text-layer-set-color.blurb: Set the color of the text in the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-color.help: This procedure sets the text color in the text layer 'layer'.

args.name: color
args.desc: The color to use for the text

gimp-text-layer-set-font.blurb: Set the font of a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-font.help: This procedure modifies the font used in the specified text layer.

gimp-text-layer-set-font-size.help: This procedure changes the font size of a text layer. The size of your font will be a double 'font-size' of 'unit' units.

args.name: unit
args.desc: The unit to use for the font size

gimp-text-layer-set-hint-style.blurb: Control how font outlines are hinted in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-hint-style.help: This procedure sets the hint style for font outlines in a text layer. This controls whether to fit font outlines to the pixel grid, and if so, whether to optimize for fidelity or contrast.

args.name: style
args.desc: The new hint style { TEXT-HINT-STYLE-NONE (0), TEXT-HINT-STYLE-SLIGHT (1), TEXT-HINT-STYLE-MEDIUM (2), TEXT-HINT-STYLE-FULL (3) }

gimp-text-layer-set-hinting.blurb: Enable/disable the use of hinting in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-hinting.help:
This procedure enables or disables hinting on the text of a text layer. If you enable 'auto-hint', FreeType's automatic hinter will be used and hinting information from the font will be ignored.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-text-layer-set-hint-style' instead.

args.name: hinting
args.desc: Enable/disable the use of hinting on the text (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: autohint
args.desc: Force the use of the autohinter provided through FreeType (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-text-layer-set-indent.blurb: Set the indentation of the first line in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-indent.help: This procedure sets the indentation of the first line in the text layer.

args.name: indent
args.desc: The indentation for the first line. (-8192 <= indent <= 8192)

gimp-text-layer-set-justification.blurb: Set the justification of the text in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-justification.help: This procedure sets the alignment of the lines in the text layer relative to each other.

args.name: justify
args.desc: The justification for your text. { TEXT-JUSTIFY-LEFT (0), TEXT-JUSTIFY-RIGHT (1), TEXT-JUSTIFY-CENTER (2), TEXT-JUSTIFY-FILL (3) }

gimp-text-layer-set-kerning.blurb: Enable/disable kerning in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-kerning.help: This procedure enables or disables kerning in a text layer.

args.name: kerning
args.desc: Enable/disable kerning in the text (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-text-layer-set-language.blurb: Set the language of the text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-language.help: This procedure sets the language of the text in text layer. For some scripts the language has an influence of how the text is rendered.

args.name: language
args.desc: The new language to use for the text layer

gimp-text-layer-set-letter-spacing.blurb: Adjust the letter spacing in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-letter-spacing.help: This procedure sets the additional spacing between the single glyphs in a text layer.

args.name: letter-spacing
args.desc: The additional letter spacing to use. (-8192 <= letter-spacing <= 8192)

gimp-text-layer-set-line-spacing.blurb: Adjust the line spacing in a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-line-spacing.help: This procedure sets the additional spacing used between lines a text layer.

args.name: line-spacing
args.desc: The additional line spacing to use. (-8192 <= line-spacing <= 8192)

gimp-text-layer-set-text.blurb: Set the text of a text layer.
gimp-text-layer-set-text.help: This procedure changes the text of a text layer.

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: source-x
args.desc: X coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: source-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: scale-x
args.desc: Amount to scale in x direction

args.name: scale-y
args.desc: Amount to scale in y direction

args.name: dest-x
args.desc: X coordinate of where the centre goes

args.name: dest-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of where the centre goes

gimp-undo-push-group-end.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-end' instead.
gimp-undo-push-group-end.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-end' instead.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to close an undo group

gimp-undo-push-group-start.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-start' instead.
gimp-undo-push-group-start.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-start' instead.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to open an undo group

gimp-unit-get-abbreviation.blurb: Returns the abbreviation of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-abbreviation.help: This procedure returns the abbreviation of the unit ("in" for inches).

gimp-unit-get-deletion-flag.blurb: Returns the deletion flag of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-deletion-flag.help: This procedure returns the deletion flag of the unit. If this value is TRUE the unit's definition will not be saved in the user's unitrc file on gimp exit.

gimp-unit-get-digits.blurb: Returns the number of digits of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-digits.help: This procedure returns the number of digits you should provide in input or output functions to get approximately the same accuracy as with two digits and inches. Note that asking for the digits of "pixels" will produce an error.

gimp-unit-get-factor.blurb: Returns the factor of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-factor.help: This procedure returns the unit's factor which indicates how many units make up an inch. Note that asking for the factor of "pixels" will produce an error.

gimp-unit-get-identifier.blurb: Returns the textual identifier of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-identifier.help: This procedure returns the textual identifier of the unit. For built-in units it will be the english singular form of the unit's name. For user-defined units this should equal to the singular form.

gimp-unit-get-number-of-built-in-units.blurb: Returns the number of built-in units.
gimp-unit-get-number-of-built-in-units.help: This procedure returns the number of defined units built-in to GIMP.

gimp-unit-get-number-of-units.help: This procedure returns the number of defined units.

gimp-unit-get-plural.blurb: Returns the plural form of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-plural.help: This procedure returns the plural form of the unit.

gimp-unit-get-singular.blurb: Returns the singular form of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-singular.help: This procedure returns the singular form of the unit.

gimp-unit-get-symbol.blurb: Returns the symbol of the unit.
gimp-unit-get-symbol.help: This procedure returns the symbol of the unit ("''" for inches).

gimp-unit-new.blurb: Creates a new unit and returns it's integer ID.
gimp-unit-new.help: This procedure creates a new unit and returns it's integer ID. Note that the new unit will have it's deletion flag set to TRUE, so you will have to set it to FALSE with 'gimp-unit-set-deletion-flag' to make it persistent.

gimp-unit-set-deletion-flag.blurb: Sets the deletion flag of a unit.
gimp-unit-set-deletion-flag.help: This procedure sets the unit's deletion flag. If the deletion flag of a unit is TRUE on gimp exit, this unit's definition will not be saved in the user's unitrc.

args.name: deletion-flag
args.desc: The new deletion flag of the unit (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-conicto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a conic bezier spline.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-conicto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a conic bezier spline. Actually a cubic bezier spline gets added that realizes the shape of a conic bezier spline.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the control point

args.name: y0
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the control point

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the end point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the end point

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-cubicto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a cubic bezier spline.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-cubicto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a cubic bezier spline.

args.name: x0
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the first control point

args.name: y0
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the first control point

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the second control point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the second control point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x-coordinate of the end point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y-coordinate of the end point

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-lineto.blurb: Extends a bezier stroke with a lineto.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-lineto.help: Extends a bezier stroke with a lineto.

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-ellipse.blurb: Adds a bezier stroke describing an ellipse the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-ellipse.help: Adds a bezier stroke describing an ellipse the vectors object.

args.name: angle
args.desc: The angle the x-axis of the ellipse (radians, counterclockwise)

gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-moveto.blurb: Adds a bezier stroke with a single moveto to the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-bezier-stroke-new-moveto.help: Adds a bezier stroke with a single moveto to the vectors object.

gimp-vectors-copy.help: This procedure copies the specified vectors object and returns the copy.

gimp-vectors-export-to-file.blurb: save a path as an SVG file.
gimp-vectors-export-to-file.help: This procedure creates an SVG file to save a Vectors object, that is, a path. The resulting file can be edited using a vector graphics application, or later reloaded into GIMP. If you pass 0 as the 'vectors' argument, then all paths in the image will be exported.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the SVG file to create.

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to be saved, or 0 for all in the image

gimp-vectors-export-to-string.blurb: Save a path as an SVG string.
gimp-vectors-export-to-string.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-vectors-export-to-file' but creates a string rather than a file. The contents are a NUL-terminated string that holds a complete XML document. If you pass 0 as the 'vectors' argument, then all paths in the image will be exported.

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to save, or 0 for all in the image

gimp-vectors-get-image.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-image.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-image' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-linked.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-strokes.blurb: List the strokes associated with the passed path.
gimp-vectors-get-strokes.help: Returns an Array with the stroke-IDs associated with the passed path.

gimp-vectors-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-vectors-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-vectors-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-vectors-import-from-file.blurb: Import paths from an SVG file.
gimp-vectors-import-from-file.help: This procedure imports paths from an SVG file. SVG elements other than paths and basic shapes are ignored.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the SVG file to import.

args.name: merge
args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-import-from-string.blurb: Import paths from an SVG string.
gimp-vectors-import-from-string.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-vectors-import-from-file' but takes a string rather than reading the SVG from a file. This allows you to write scripts that generate SVG and feed it to GIMP.

args.name: string
args.desc: A string that must be a complete and valid SVG document.

args.name: length
args.desc: Number of bytes in string or -1 if the string is NULL terminated.

args.name: merge
args.desc: Merge paths into a single vectors object. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: scale
args.desc: Scale the SVG to image dimensions. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-is-valid.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.
gimp-vectors-is-valid.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-is-valid' instead.

gimp-vectors-new.help: Creates a new empty vectors object. The vectors object needs to be added to the image using 'gimp-image-insert-vectors'.

args.desc: the name of the new vector object.

gimp-vectors-new-from-text-layer.blurb: Creates a new vectors object from a text layer.
gimp-vectors-new-from-text-layer.help: Creates a new vectors object from a text layer. The vectors object needs to be added to the image using 'gimp-image-insert-vectors'.

gimp-vectors-parasite-attach.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-attach-parasite' instead.

args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-vectors-remove-stroke.blurb: remove the stroke from a vectors object.
gimp-vectors-remove-stroke.help: Remove the stroke from a vectors object.

gimp-vectors-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-vectors-stroke-flip.help: Rotates the given stroke around given center by angle (in degrees).

args.name: flip-type
args.desc: Flip orientation, either vertical or horizontal { ORIENTATION-HORIZONTAL (0), ORIENTATION-VERTICAL (1) }

args.name: axis
args.desc: axis coordinate about which to flip, in pixels

gimp-vectors-stroke-flip-free.blurb: flips the given stroke about an arbitrary axis.
gimp-vectors-stroke-flip-free.help: Flips the given stroke about an arbitrary axis. Axis is defined by two coordinates in the image (in pixels), through which the flipping axis passes.

args.name: x1
args.desc: X coordinate of the first point of the flipping axis

args.name: y1
args.desc: Y coordinate of the first point of the flipping axis

args.name: x2
args.desc: X coordinate of the second point of the flipping axis

args.name: y2
args.desc: Y coordinate of the second point of the flipping axis

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-length.blurb: Measure the length of the given stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-length.help: Measure the length of the given stroke.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-point-at-dist.blurb: Get point at a specified distance along the stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-point-at-dist.help: This will return the x,y position of a point at a given distance along the stroke. The distance will be obtained by first digitizing the curve internally and then walking along the curve. For a closed stroke the start of the path is the first point on the path that was created. This might not be obvious. If the stroke is not long enough, a "valid" flag will be FALSE.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-get-points.blurb: returns the control points of a stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-get-points.help: returns the control points of a stroke. The interpretation of the coordinates returned depends on the type of the stroke. For Gimp 2.4 this is always a bezier stroke, where the coordinates are the control points.

gimp-vectors-stroke-interpolate.blurb: returns polygonal approximation of the stroke.
gimp-vectors-stroke-interpolate.help: returns polygonal approximation of the stroke.

args.name: precision
args.desc: The precision used for the approximation

gimp-vectors-stroke-new-from-points.blurb: Adds a stroke of a given type to the vectors object.
gimp-vectors-stroke-new-from-points.help: Adds a stroke of a given type to the vectors object. The coordinates of the control points can be specified. For now only strokes of the type GIMP_VECTORS_STROKE_TYPE_BEZIER are supported. The control points are specified as a pair of float values for the x- and y-coordinate. The Bezier stroke type needs a multiple of three control points. Each Bezier segment endpoint (anchor, A) has two additional control points (C) associated. They are specified in the order CACCACCAC...

args.name: type
args.desc: type of the stroke (always GIMP_VECTORS_STROKE_TYPE_BEZIER for now). { VECTORS-STROKE-TYPE-BEZIER (0) }

args.name: num-points
args.desc: The number of elements in the array, i.e. the number of controlpoints in the stroke * 2 (x- and y-coordinate). (num-points >= 0)

args.name: controlpoints
args.desc: List of the x- and y-coordinates of the control points.

args.name: closed
args.desc: Whether the stroke is to be closed or not. (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-vectors-stroke-rotate.help: Rotates the given stroke around given center by angle (in degrees).

args.name: center-x
args.desc: X coordinate of the rotation center

args.name: center-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of the rotation center

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object to render to the selection

args.name: operation
args.desc: The desired operation with current selection { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

gimp-version.help: This procedure returns the version number of the currently running GIMP.

gimp-xcf-load.blurb: Loads file saved in the .xcf file format
gimp-xcf-load.help: The XCF file format has been designed specifically for loading and saving tiled and layered images in GIMP. This procedure will load the specified file.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load, in the on-disk character set and encoding

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The basename of the file, in UTF-8

gimp-xcf-save.blurb: Saves file in the .xcf file format
gimp-xcf-save.help: The XCF file format has been designed specifically for loading and saving tiled and layered images in GIMP. This procedure will save the specified image in the xcf file format.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in, in URI format and UTF-8 encoding

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The basename of the file, in UTF-8

plug-in-alienmap2.help: No help yet. Just try it and you'll see!

args.name: link-after-alignment
args.desc: Link the visible layers after alignment { TRUE, FALSE }

plug-in-animationoptimize.blurb: Modify image to reduce size when saved as GIF animation
plug-in-animationoptimize.help: This procedure applies various optimizations to a GIMP layer-based animation in an attempt to reduce the final file size.  If a frame of the animation can use the 'combine' mode, this procedure attempts to maximize the number of ajdacent pixels having the same color, which improves the compression for some image formats such as GIF or MNG.

plug-in-animationoptimize-diff.blurb: Reduce file size where combining layers is possible
plug-in-animationoptimize-diff.help: This procedure applies various optimizations to a GIMP layer-based animation in an attempt to reduce the final file size.  If a frame of the animation can use the 'combine' mode, this procedure uses a simple difference between the frames.

plug-in-animationunoptimize.blurb: Remove optimization to make editing easier
plug-in-animationunoptimize.help: This procedure 'simplifies' a GIMP layer-based animation that has been optimized for animation. This makes editing the animation much easier.

plug-in-antialias.blurb: Antialias using the Scale3X edge-extrapolation algorithm

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-apply-canvas.help: This function applies a canvas texture map to the drawable.

args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-applylens.blurb: Simulate an elliptical lens over the image
plug-in-applylens.help: This plug-in uses Snell's law to draw an ellipsoid lens over the image.

args.name: set-transparent
args.desc: Set lens surroundings transparent (TRUE or FALSE)

plug-in-autocrop.blurb: Remove empty borders from the image
plug-in-autocrop.help: Remove empty borders from the image.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-autocrop-layer.blurb: Crop the active layer based on empty borders of the input drawable
plug-in-autocrop-layer.help:
Crop the active layer of the input "image" based on empty borders of the input "drawable". 

The input drawable serves as a base for detecting cropping extents (transparency or background color), and is not necessarily the cropped layer (the current active layer).

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-autostretch-hsv.blurb: Stretch contrast to cover the maximum possible range
plug-in-autostretch-hsv.help: This simple plug-in does an automatic contrast stretch. For each channel in the image, it finds the minimum and maximum values... it uses those values to stretch the individual histograms to the full contrast range. For some images it may do just what you want; for others it may be total crap :). This version differs from Contrast Autostretch in that it works in HSV space, and preserves hue.

args.name: run-mode

plug-in-c-astretch.blurb: Stretch contrast to cover the maximum possible range
plug-in-c-astretch.help: This simple plug-in does an automatic contrast stretch. For each channel in the image, it finds the minimum and maximum values... it uses those values to stretch the individual histograms to the full contrast range. For some images it may do just what you want; for others it may not work that well.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-cartoon.blurb: Simulate a cartoon by enhancing edges
plug-in-cartoon.help: Propagates dark values in an image based on each pixel's relative darkness to a neighboring average. The idea behind this filter is to give the look of a black felt pen drawing subsequently shaded with color. This is achieved by darkening areas of the image which are measured to be darker than a neighborhood average. In this way, sufficiently large shifts in intensity are darkened to black. The rate at which they are darkened to black is determined by the second pct_black parameter. The mask_radius parameter controls the size of the pixel neighborhood over which the average intensity is computed and then compared to each pixel in the neighborhood to decide whether or not to darken it to black. Large values for mask_radius result in very thick black areas bordering the shaded regions of color and much less detail for black areas everywhere including inside regions of color. Small values result in more subtle pen strokes and detail everywhere. Small values for the pct_black make the blend from the color regions to the black border lines smoother and the lines themselves thinner and less noticeable; larger values achieve the opposite effect.

args.name: mask-radius
args.desc: Cartoon mask radius (radius of pixel neighborhood)

args.name: pct-black
args.desc: Percentage of darkened pixels to set to black (0.0 - 1.0)

plug-in-ccanalyze.blurb: Analyze the set of colors in the image
plug-in-ccanalyze.help: Analyze colorcube and print some information about the current image (also displays a color-histogram)

plug-in-cml-explorer.help: Make an image of Coupled-Map Lattice (CML). CML is a kind of Cellula Automata on continuous (value) domain. In GIMP_RUN_NONINTERACTIVE, the name of a parameter file is passed as the 4th arg. You can control CML_explorer via parameter file.

args.name: parameter-filename
args.desc: The name of parameter file. CML_explorer makes an image with its settings.

plug-in-color-enhance.blurb: Stretch color saturation to cover maximum possible range
plug-in-color-enhance.help: This simple plug-in does an automatic saturation stretch.  For each channel in the image, it finds the minimum and maximum values... it uses those values to stretch the individual histograms to the full range.  For some images it may do just what you want; for others it may not work that well.  This version differs from Contrast Autostretch in that it works in HSV space, and preserves hue.

plug-in-curve-bend.blurb: Bend the image using two control curves
plug-in-curve-bend.help: This plug-in does bend the active layer If there is a current selection it is copied to floating selection and the curve_bend distortion is done on the floating selection. If work_on_copy parameter is TRUE, the curve_bend distortion is done on a copy of the active layer (or floating selection). The upper and lower edges are bent in shape of 2 spline curves. both (upper and lower) curves are determined by upto 17 points or by 256 Y-Values if curve_type == 1 (freehand mode) If rotation is not 0, the layer is rotated before and rotated back after the bend operation. This enables bending in other directions than vertical. bending usually changes the size of the handled layer. this plug-in sets the offsets of the handled layer to keep its center at the same position

args.name: drawable
args.desc: Input drawable (must be a layer without layermask)

args.name: rotation
args.desc: Direction {angle 0 to 360 degree } of the bend effect

args.name: work-on-copy
args.desc: { TRUE, FALSE } TRUE: copy the drawable and bend the copy

args.name: upper-point-x
args.desc: array of 17 x point_koords { 0.0 <= x <= 1.0 or -1 for unused point }

args.name: upper-point-y
args.desc: array of 17 y point_koords { 0.0 <= y <= 1.0 or -1 for unused point }

args.name: lower-point-x
args.desc: array of 17 x point_koords { 0.0 <= x <= 1.0 or -1 for unused point }

args.name: lower-point-y

args.name: lower-val-y

plug-in-photocopy.blurb: Simulate color distortion produced by a copy machine
plug-in-photocopy.help: Propagates dark values in an image based on each pixel's relative darkness to a neighboring average. The idea behind this filter is to give the look of a photocopied version of the image, with toner transferred based on the relative darkness of a particular region. This is achieved by darkening areas of the image which are measured to be darker than a neighborhood average and setting other pixels to white. In this way, sufficiently large shifts in intensity are darkened to black. The rate at which they are darkened to black is determined by the second pct_black parameter. The mask_radius parameter controls the size of the pixel neighborhood over which the average intensity is computed and then compared to each pixel in the neighborhood to decide whether or not to darken it to black. Large values for mask_radius result in very thick black areas bordering the regions of white and much less detail for black areas everywhere including inside regions of color. Small values result in less toner overall and more detail everywhere. Small values for the pct_black make the blend from the white regions to the black border lines smoother and the toner regions themselves thinner and less noticeable; larger values achieve the opposite effect.

args.name: mask-radius
args.desc: Photocopy mask radius (radius of pixel neighborhood)

args.name: pct-black
args.desc: Percentage of darkened pixels to set to black (0.0 - 1.0)

args.name: pct-white
args.desc: Percentage of non-darkened pixels left white (0.0 - 1.0)

plug-in-sample-colorize.blurb: Colorize image using a sample image as a guide
plug-in-sample-colorize.help: This plug-in colorizes the contents of the specified (gray) layer with the help of a  sample (color) layer. It analyzes all colors in the sample layer. The sample colors are sorted by brightness (== intentisty) and amount and stored in a sample colortable (where brightness is the index) The pixels of the destination layer are remapped with the help of the sample colortable. If use_subcolors is TRUE, the remapping process uses all sample colors of the corresponding brightness-intensity and distributes the subcolors according to their amount in the sample (If the sample has 5 green, 3 yellow, and 1 red pixel of the  intensity value 105, the destination pixels at intensity value 105 are randomly painted in green, yellow and red in a relation of 5:3:1 If use_subcolors is FALSE only one sample color per intensity is used. (green will be used in this example) The brightness intensity value is transformed at the remapping process according to the levels: out_lo, out_hi, in_lo, in_high and gamma The in_low / in_high levels specify an initial mapping of the intensity. The gamma value determines how intensities are interpolated between the in_lo and in_high levels. A gamma value of 1.0 results in linear interpolation. Higher gamma values results in more high-level intensities Lower gamma values results in more low-level intensities The out_low/out_high levels constrain the resulting intensity index The intensity index is used to pick the corresponding color in the sample colortable. If hold_inten is FALSE the picked color is used 1:1 as resulting remap_color. If hold_inten is TRUE The brightness of the picked color is adjusted back to the origial intensity value (only hue and saturation are taken from the picked sample color) (or to the input level, if orig_inten is set FALSE) Works on both Grayscale and RGB image with/without alpha channel. (the image with the dst_drawable is converted to RGB if necessary) The sample_drawable should be of type RGB or RGBA

args.name: dst-drawable
args.desc: The drawable to be colorized (Type GRAY* or RGB*)

args.name: sample-drawable
args.desc: Sample drawable (should be of Type RGB or RGBA)

args.name: orig-inten
args.desc: TRUE: hold brightness of original intensity levels. FALSE: Hold Intensity of input levels

args.name: rnd-subcolors
args.desc: TRUE: Use all subcolors of same intensity, FALSE: use only one color per intensity

args.name: guess-missing
args.desc: TRUE: guess samplecolors for the missing intensity values FALSE: use only colors found in the sample

args.name: gamma
args.desc: gamma adjustment factor (0.1 <= gamma <= 10) where 1.0 is linear

The last four parameters are optional and can be used to specify the corners of the region to be grabbed.On Mac OS X or on gnome-shell, when called non-interactively, the plug-inonly can take screenshots of the entire root window.Grabbing a window or a region is not supportednon-interactively. To grab a region or a particularwindow, you need to use the interactive mode.

plug-in-sharpen.help: This plug-in selectively performs a convolution filter on an image.

plug-in-shift.blurb: Shift each row or column of pixels by a random amount
plug-in-shift.help: Shifts the pixels of the specified drawable. Each row or column will be displaced a random value of pixels.

plug-in-small-tiles.blurb: Tile image into smaller versions of the original

plug-in-smooth-palette.blurb: Derive a smooth color palette from the image

plug-in-sobel.help: This plug-in calculates the gradient with a sobel operator. The user can specify which direction to use. When both directions are used, the result is the RMS of the two gradients; if only one direction is used, the result either the absolute value of the gradient, or 127 + gradient (if the 'keep sign' switch is on). This way, information about the direction of the gradient is preserved. Resulting images are not autoscaled."

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

args.name: horizontal
args.desc: Sobel in horizontal direction (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: vertical
args.desc: Sobel in vertical direction (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: keep-sign
args.desc: Keep sign of result (one direction only) (TRUE or FALSE)

plug-in-softglow.blurb: Simulate glow by making highlights intense and fuzzy
plug-in-softglow.help: Gives an image a softglow effect by intensifying the highlights in the image. This is done by screening a modified version of the drawable with itself. The modified version is desaturated and then a sigmoidal transfer function is applied to force the distribution of intensities into very small and very large only. This desaturated version is then blurred to give it a fuzzy 'vaseline-on-the-lens' effect. The glow radius parameter controls the sharpness of the glow effect. The brightness parameter controls the degree of intensification applied to image highlights. The sharpness parameter controls how defined or alternatively, diffuse, the glow effect should be.

plug-in-solid-noise.help: Generates 2D textures using Perlin's classic solid noise function.

plug-in-sparkle.blurb: Turn bright spots into starry sparkles

plug-in-spheredesigner.blurb: Create an image of a textured sphere
plug-in-spheredesigner.help: This plug-in can be used to create textured and/or bumpmapped spheres, and uses a small lightweight raytracer to perform the task with good quality

plug-in-spread.help: Spreads the pixels of the specified drawable. Pixels are randomly moved to another location whose distance varies from the original by the horizontal and vertical spread amounts.

plug-in-tile.blurb: Create an array of copies of the image
plug-in-tile.help: This function creates a new image with a single layer sized to the specified 'new_width' and 'new_height' parameters.  The specified drawable is tiled into this layer.  The new layer will have the same type as the specified drawable and the new image will have a corresponding base type.

plug-in-unit-editor.blurb: Create or alter units used in GIMP

plug-in-unsharp-mask.blurb: The most widely useful method for sharpening an image
plug-in-unsharp-mask.help: The unsharp mask is a sharpening filter that works by comparing using the difference of the image and a blurred version of the image. It is commonly used on photographic images, and is provides a much more pleasing result than the standard sharpen filter.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-video.blurb: Simulate distortion produced by a fuzzy or low-res monitor
plug-in-video.help: This function simulates the degradation of being on an old low-dotpitch RGB video monitor to the specified drawable.

plug-in-vinvert.blurb: Invert the brightness of each pixel
plug-in-vinvert.help: This function takes an indexed/RGB image and inverts its 'value' in HSV space. The upshot of this is that the color and saturation at any given point remains the same, but its brightness is effectively inverted. Quite strange. Sometimes produces unpleasant color artifacts on images from lossy sources (ie. JPEG).

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

plug-in-warp.blurb: Twist or smear image in many different ways

plug-in-whirl-pinch.blurb: Distort an image by whirling and pinching
plug-in-whirl-pinch.help: Distorts the image by whirling and pinching, which are two common center-based, circular distortions. Whirling is like projecting the image onto the surface of water in a toilet and flushing. Pinching is similar to projecting the image onto an elastic surface and pressing or pulling on the center of the surface.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: The run mode { RUN-INTERACTIVE (0), RUN-NONINTERACTIVE (1), RUN-WITH-LAST-VALS (2) }

args.desc: Radius (1.0 is the largest circle that fits in the image, and 2.0 goes all the way to the corners) (0 <= radius <= 2)
